It claims a single charged shaft and answers intrusion with striking with branching lightning at the best-grounded target. Toward grey-folk it is drawn to metal and wet crews; survey crews report that Wall-Wardens strip and stow metal in any gallery that smells of ozone. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
c.traitsText: Stable characters of Arc Stormling: a body of charged storm-air veined with living electrical discharge; immune to electricity and to physical attack; and a defining weakness — grounding and damp enclosure, which bleed off its charge. Its senses run through the medium itself: electrical-field sensing; it strikes the best ground. It tolerates darkness, heat, and thin air but fails at grounding-out and damp enclosure. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the charged cloud and arc-veined surface, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
c.abilities: Abilities of Arc Stormling are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it strikes with branching lightning at grounded targets; it builds charge to a stunning discharge; it draws charged air to rebuild its body; and it crawls arcs along metal to reach distant prey. Each is a direct expression of living electrical charge in storm-air, so it cannot, for instance, produce mass-force, persist when grounded out, or cross dead damp air. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
c.bg: In the Throat, Arc Stormling matters because it makes ozone-smelling galleries a lightning hazard, and crews now stow metal before entering. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as an electrical-strike hazard logged with charged shafts, and its presence reshapes routes through the upper charged shafts, now entered metal-stowed and on insulated footing. Rising hair and crawling arcs without a storm are the Wall-Wardens' sign a Stormling has formed.

c.g: Static Fulgurants is the family-rank grouping of clinging surface-charge elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a clinging static body that crawls and shocks on contact, and divide below into genus groups by charge build and mote size. The crawling contact-shock of dry galleries.
c.tax: Placed at family rank, Static Fulgurants is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a clinging surface-static body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a clinging charged body.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a faint glowing mote on surfaces. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a fizzing static residue — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Static Fulgurants occupies dry charged galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure animates surface static and recur with the gallery re-charges, sustaining themselves on surface static charge.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: crawls along conductors and shocks contact. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: clinging mote and surface-charge sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: contact-shock and conductor-crawl. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Static Fulgurants matters because rides gear to shock the hand. It lets survey reports sort clinging charge hazards from striking ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Sparkmote is the genus-rank grouping of clinging-static mote elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a clinging static body that crawls and shocks on contact, and divide below into species groups by charge build and mote size. A crawling spark that rides your gear.
c.tax: Placed at genus rank, Sparkmote is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a clinging static-mote body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a clinging surface-static body.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a faint glowing static mote. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a fizzing residue and scorch-spots — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Sparkmote occupies dry charged galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure animates surface static and recur with the gallery re-charges, sustaining themselves on surface static charge.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: crawls onto gear and shocks contact. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: clinging mote and charge-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: contact-shock and conductor-crawl. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Sparkmote matters because shocks the hand via the rope. It lets survey reports sort clinging-mote forms from striking-cloud ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Static Sparkmote (Fulgur repens) is a small storm-air elemental of clinging static that crawls over surfaces and shocks on contact. Documented from the dry charged galleries where surface static builds, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a drifting mote of glowing static that clings and crawls over dry surfaces — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Where the Stormling strikes from the air, the Sparkmote crawls along walls and gear, shocking whatever it touches. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a fading glow and a last weak crackle as the charge bleeds away and no carcass.
c.tax: Static Sparkmote is placed at species rank beneath Sparkmote (Fulgurant order, Air Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a clinging-static body, crawling surface discharge, and contact-shock as its harm. It is separated from its sibling the Arc Stormling by its clinging crawling surface static versus the Stormling's branching air-strikes; the two share the genus body-plan but never the branching air-strike. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: surface static charge held in a small drifting mote.
c.ap: Static Sparkmote stands 0.2–0.5 m glowing mote. Its silhouette is a small glowing ball of static that flattens and crawls on surfaces. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a faint blue glow clinging to walls, ropes, and gear; a continuous fizzing crackle rather than a single strike; and small hairs and dust standing up wherever it crawls. It moves with a crawling drift over dry surfaces, hopping between conductors, and shows a faint clinging blue glow. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a fizzing residue and small scorch-spots where it clung, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
c.eco: Static Sparkmote persists in dry charged galleries, dust shafts with static build-up, and rope-rigged passages where charge clings. It does not breed; it forms where air-pressure animates a surface-static build-up into a drifting mote, and recurs whenever the dry gallery re-charges and the mote re-forms. It sustains its mass on surface static charge, drawing in static drawn from dry charged surfaces. Range is bounded by the dry charged zone: outside that band the body bleeds to ground and fades in damp air. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a dry gallery where gear crackles and dust stands up with no storm, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
c.beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Static Sparkmote is roused by contact with skin, rope, or gear and otherwise lies a faint glow clinging to a wall, easily missed. It claims a single dry charged gallery and answers intrusion with crawling onto and shocking whatever touches its surface. Toward grey-folk it is a clinging contact hazard; survey crews report that Lanternkin keep ropes dry-waxed, since a Sparkmote rides damp lines to the hand. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
c.traitsText: Stable characters of Static Sparkmote: a body of surface static charge held in a small drifting mote; immune to electricity and to physical attack; and a defining weakness — damp and grounding, which discharge it harmlessly. Its senses run through the medium itself: surface-charge sensing along conductors. It tolerates darkness, heat, and dryness but fails at damp and grounding. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the clinging mote and crawling discharge, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
c.abilities: Abilities of Static Sparkmote are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it shocks on contact with skin, rope, or metal; it crawls along conductors to reach prey; it draws surface static to renew itself; and it clings unseen to gear until touched. Each is a direct expression of clinging surface static charge, so it cannot, for instance, strike at range, produce mass-force, or persist in damp air. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
c.bg: In the Throat, Static Sparkmote matters because it rides ropes and gear to deliver a shock at the hand, and crews now keep lines dry-waxed. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a contact-shock hazard logged with dry charged galleries, and its presence reshapes routes through the dry charged galleries, now rigged with dry-waxed lines and insulated grips. Gear that crackles and dust that stands up in a dry gallery mark a clinging Sparkmote.

c.g: Quasi-elementals are inner-planar organisms formed where elemental states mix, shear, or contaminate one another. They are not lesser true elementals but boundary creatures of steam, magma, dust, ash, salt, radiance, vacuum, and similar transitional substances.
c.tax: Phylum subordinate to Elementals. Membership is assigned by mixed or para-elemental composition rather than by a single pure element.
c.ap: Most quasi-elementals show unstable margins: vapor venting from skin, dust-shedding joints, molten seams, or condensation halos. Their bodies often require surrounding medium to remain coherent.
c.eco: They reproduce or recur where elemental borders are turbulent: vent fields, ashfalls, boiling pools, collapsing caverns, and planar seams.
c.beh: Behavior is volatile and boundary-seeking; mephits are the chattering, social, minor order of the clade.
c.traitsText: Mixed elemental body, environmental dependence, resistance matching constituent element, and instability under opposed conditions.
c.abilities: Minor elemental exhalations, heat/cold/acid/dust effects, and short bursts of planar transition.
c.bg: In the Throat they mark places where the inner planes leak into geology rather than simply places where ordinary matter is hot, wet, cold, or windy.

c.g: Genie Princes is the phylum-rank grouping of intelligent planar elemental nobles native to the four Inner Planes of Earth, Fire, Air, and Water within the True Elementals. Its members share a planar origin (not material-world geological formation), a humanoid body of elemental material, wish-granting architecture inherited from the Jann stem, and a territorial structure organised around planar claims rather than geological faults, and divide below into class groups by the primary element of the body and planar origin: Earth (Dao), Fire (Efreeti), Air (Djinn), or Water (Marid). The Genie Princes phylum is the journal's formal placement for the four genie noble lineages. They are not large or intelligent True Elementals — their planar origin, wish-granting architecture, and non-geological formation place them in a distinct phylum beneath the Elementals kingdom.
c.tax: Placed at phylum rank, Genie Princes is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin intelligent elemental noble with wish-granting architecture; formed in an Inner Plane, not by material-world geological pressure. A new class belongs here only if it shows a planar-origin humanoid elemental noble with wish-granting architecture and an Inner Plane origin.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by four distinct body-plans: dark metallic stone (Dao), brass over flame (Efreeti), translucent moving air (Djinn), and living ocean water with bioluminescent currents (Marid). A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — each class has a definitive non-geological field sign: metallic inclusions and shifting gem-glow (Dao), no char-ring with metallic grip-marks and sulphur (Efreeti), wind-direction anomaly against the geological draught (Djinn), and bioluminescent blue-green water (Marid) — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Genie Princes occupies claimed material-world mineral deposits, volcanic zones, storm shafts, and deep underwater territories; native to the four Inner Planes. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Inner Planes; not formed by material-world geological processes and recur with return to their Inner Plane when killed; do not re-form at a material site, sustaining themselves on planar elemental will from the Inner Planes.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: intelligent, territorial, contractual, and wish-granting; all classes offer negotiation before force, and all classes enforce territory through planar means rather than geological ones. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: a planar-origin elemental body, wish-granting architecture, Inner Plane origin, and a distinctive non-geological field sign specific to each class. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each class and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: wish-granting (up to three, precisely interpreted), element-travel, territory-claiming and enforcement, and elemental-scale effects. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Genie Princes matters because their presence in the material Gorge is indicated by non-geological field signs: wrong-direction geological forces, metallic inclusions in elementals, no-char-ring fire entities, wind anomalies, and bioluminescent pools. It lets survey reports sort genie nobles from True Elementals and Quasi-Elementals by their planar origin and wish-granting architecture without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Jann (Spiritus inaequalis) is the theorised undifferentiated genie ancestor-form, inferred from shared structural properties of the four genie classes: an elemental-planar body that has not yet committed to a single element. Documented from no specimen; inferred from shared traits of Dao, Efreeti, Djinn, and Marid, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — an inferred uncommitted elemental-planar body-matrix — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Unlike the true elemental Proto-Elementals (also theorised), the Jann is placed here as the stem form of the genie lineage rather than the elemental one — it is inferred from the shared planar constitution, shared intelligence, and shared wish-granting structure of the four classes. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves none; no specimen ever recorded and no carcass.
c.tax: Jann is placed at species rank beneath (stem, inferred) ((Genie Princes, stem) order, Genie Princes class). Diagnosis rests on the shared planar-body architecture, uncommitted-element matrix, and intelligence-bearing form inferred from the four committed classes. It is separated from its sibling the four committed genie classes by its uncommitted element-matrix versus the fixed single-element planar body of each class; the two share the genus body-plan but never the single-element commitment. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: an inferred uncommitted elemental-planar matrix, not committed to earth, fire, water, or air.
c.ap: Jann stands unknown; inferred from the four class sizes as intermediate. Its silhouette is reconstructed as a semi-stable elemental-planar humanoid in an uncommitted transitional state. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: no field marker; the entry is a reconstruction; inferred instability resolving toward one element under local planar pressure; and inferred intelligence-bearing architecture shared with all four classes. It moves with not observed, and shows not observed. Even after dispersal it can be identified from none, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
c.eco: Jann persists in no habitat; the form is inferred as the common planar-body state prior to the Dao/Efreeti/Djinn/Marid commitment. It does not breed; it forms where theorised to arise where raw planar pressure has not yet committed to one element, and recurs whenever not observed; inferred to resolve rapidly into a committed class under planar conditions. It sustains its mass on raw uncommitted planar-elemental pressure, drawing in inferred to draw on whatever element it resolves toward. Range is bounded by not applicable: outside that band the body theorised to commit immediately to the locally dominant element. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as not observed, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
c.beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Jann is roused by local planar pressure, inferred to force element-commitment and otherwise lies not observed. It claims not applicable and answers intrusion with inferred to resolve toward the locally dominant element. Toward grey-folk it is never encountered; survey crews report that Stonesong Heralds treat the Jann as a teaching concept explaining why the four genie classes share intelligence, wish-structure, and planar constitution. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
c.traitsText: Stable characters of Jann: a body of an inferred uncommitted elemental-planar matrix, not committed to earth, fire, water, or air; inferred shared resistance from the four committed classes; and a defining weakness — inferred instability forcing rapid element-commitment. Its senses run through the medium itself: not observed. It tolerates not applicable but fails at commitment to a single element. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the uncommitted planar-body matrix posited as the shared genie root, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
c.abilities: Abilities of Jann are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: inferred capacity to resolve toward any one element; no observed bounded abilities; inferred intelligence-bearing architecture prior to element specialisation; and none recorded. Each is a direct expression of raw uncommitted planar-elemental pressure, so it cannot, for instance, be collected, measured, or confirmed as a living body. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
c.bg: In the Throat, Jann matters because it organises the four genie classes under one inferred planar root without claiming a specimen exists. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as an inferred stem species retained for comparison, and its presence reshapes routes through no routes; a reconstruction only. The Jann stem-species is retained as an accepted inferred orphan, parallel to Proto-Elementals in the True Elementals phylum.

c.g: Dao is the class-rank grouping of earth-element genie nobles: planar entities of the Elemental Plane of Earth within the True Elementals. Its members share a dense stone body with metallic inclusions, a gem-glow that indicates tier and emotional state, articulated stone digits, and a wish-granting architecture inherited from the Jann stem, and divide below into order groups by planar standing tier: noble-house (gold inclusions, three-wish) versus ore-kin (iron inclusions, one-wish). The Dao class is the formal placement for earth-element genie nobles. They are not Terrae elementals with extra intelligence; they are a distinct planar lineage that happens to share the earth element.
c.tax: Placed at class rank, Dao is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin stone humanoid with metallic inclusions, shifting or fixed gem-glow, and wish-granting architecture; formed in the Elemental Plane, not by tectonic pressure. A new order belongs here only if it shows a planar-origin stone humanoid with metallic inclusions and wish capacity.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a broad-shouldered dark stone humanoid with metallic seam inclusions and an internal gem-glow. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — metallic inclusions (gold for noble, iron for kin) and the presence or absence of a shifting colour glow during negotiation — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Dao occupies claimed gem-seams and ore-seams in the material world; native to the Elemental Plane of Earth. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane; not formed by tectonic or fault pressure and recur with returns to the Elemental Plane when killed in the material world, sustaining themselves on planar elemental will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: contractual, territorial of claimed mineral deposits, and wish-granting under precise terms. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: dense stone body, metallic inclusions, gem-glow, articulated digits, stone-travel, and wish-granting architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each order and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: stone-reshaping, stone-travel, wish-granting, and metallic sensing. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dao matters because responsible for sealed gallery events and the source of the Forgemasters' prior-claim lease tradition. It lets survey reports sort Dao from Terrae forms and from the other genie classes without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Dao Lords is the order-rank grouping of high-tier Dao of confirmed planar-noble standing within the True Elementals. Its members share a Dao body with gold-dominant metallic inclusions and an expanded wish-granting capacity of up to three wishes, and divide below into family groups by specific gem-claim territory and wish-granting specialisation. The Dao Lords order covers Dao of confirmed planar-noble standing — those whose metallic inclusions are primarily gold and whose wish capacity is confirmed at three.
c.tax: Placed at order rank, Dao Lords is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-noble Dao with gold-dominant inclusions and three-wish architecture. A new family belongs here only if it shows a Dao with confirmed gold-dominant metallic inclusions.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a broad dark-stone humanoid with gold metallic veins and a shifting deep-amber-to-violet gem-glow. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — gold veins visible in the stone body and a violet glow during negotiation — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Dao Lords occupies claimed deep gem-seams and the Elemental Plane. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane at noble tier and recur with returns to the Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on planar elemental will at noble tier.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: contractual and territorial; opens negotiation before force. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: gold-inclusion stone body, three-wish architecture, and stone-travel ability. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: stone-reshaping, wish-granting, gem-calling, and stone-travel. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dao Lords matters because most sealed-gallery events are attributed to Lord-tier Dao protecting claimed seams. It lets survey reports sort high-tier Dao from lesser Dao kin without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Dao Noble Houses is the family-rank grouping of Dao of established noble-house standing with claimed material-world territory within the True Elementals. Its members share a Dao body with house-specific gem-inclusion pattern and established material-world gem-seam territory, and divide below into genus groups by house lineage and specific gem-seam claim. Noble house membership is a planar heritable condition, not a cultural appointment.
c.tax: Placed at family rank, Dao Noble Houses is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a noble-house Dao with an established material-world territory claim. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a Dao with a confirmed noble-house affiliation and material-world claim.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a broad dark-stone humanoid with house-specific gem-pattern inclusions. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — house-specific gem inclusion pattern distinguishable to trained observers — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Dao Noble Houses occupies claimed gem-seams and planar noble-house territories. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at noble-house tier in the Elemental Plane and recur with returns to the Elemental Plane; claim persists after return, sustaining themselves on planar noble-tier elemental will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: contractual; defends territory through sealed galleries. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: noble-house body, three-wish, stone-travel, and territory-sealing. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: stone-reshaping, wish-granting, territory-sealing, and gem-calling. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dao Noble Houses matters because the source of most sealed-gallery events in the deep Throat. It lets survey reports sort noble-house Dao from free Dao kin without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Dao is the genus-rank grouping of earth-element planar noble entities of the Dao lineage within the True Elementals. Its members share a dense multi-element stone body with metallic inclusions, a shifting gem-glow, and planar origin distinguishing them from all material-world Terrae, and divide below into species groups by power tier and planar rank, which determine size, metallic inclusion type, and wish-granting capacity. The Dao genus is a planar-origin group, not a geological one. Its members share a lineage from the Elemental Plane of Earth, not from any material-world fault.
c.tax: Placed at genus rank, Dao is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin stone humanoid with metallic inclusions and a shifting gem-glow, generated in the Elemental Plane, not formed by tectonic pressure. A new species belongs here only if it shows a planar-origin stone humanoid with metallic inclusions and wish-granting architecture.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a broad-shouldered dark-stone humanoid with metallic seam inclusions and a shifting internal gem-glow. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — metallic inclusions and shifting gem-glow visible through the body — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Dao occupies the Elemental Plane of Earth and claimed deep gem-seams in the material world. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane; not formed by tectonic pressure and recur with return to the Elemental Plane when killed; do not re-form at a material site, sustaining themselves on planar elemental will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: contractual, territorial of gem-seams, and dangerous when contract terms are violated or territory is entered without negotiation. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: dense multi-element stone body, metallic inclusions, shifting gem-glow, and wish-granting architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: stone-reshaping, stone-travel, gem-seam calling, and wish-granting. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dao matters because responsible for several sealed gallery events in the deep Throat; the source of the Forgemasters' contract caution around unclaimed gem-seams. It lets survey reports sort earth-genie entities from material-world Terrae, so a crew is not applying geological dispersal techniques to a planar-origin body without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Dao (Geniis terrae) is an earth-element planar noble: a powerful intelligence native to the Elemental Plane of Earth, taking the form of a broad-shouldered stone-dark humanoid of unusual elemental density and articulacy. Documented from the Elemental Plane of Earth and the deepest metamorphic intrusions of the material world, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a dense, articulated humanoid body of dark layered stone with metallic inclusions and a glow that shifts with emotional state — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The Dao is not a Terrae form with intelligence — its planar origin, wish-granting structure, and multi-elemental stone body (which incorporates metals as well as rock) place it in a distinct genie lineage. It does not form from fault pressure; it arrives. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a careful, stratified collapse of dark stone with metallic seams, which reconsolidates elsewhere on the elemental plane and no carcass.
c.tax: Dao is placed at species rank beneath Dao (Dao Lords order, Dao class). Diagnosis rests on a dense dark-stone humanoid with metallic inclusions, a state-shifting glow, and planar origin distinguishing it from all Terrae forms. It is separated from its sibling Terrae elementalis nobilis by its planar-origin multi-element dark-stone body with metallic inclusions and wish-granting architecture versus the nobilis's metamorphic-rock fold-pressure body confined to material-world faults; the two share the genus body-plan but never the material-world fault formation. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: dense layered dark stone with gold, silver, and iron-ore inclusions, animated by planar elemental will rather than tectonic pressure.
c.ap: Dao stands 3–5 m, typically broader than a Terrae form of similar height, with a more defined face-mass. Its silhouette is a powerfully built stone humanoid with articulated digits (like the nobilis Terrae), metallic vein inclusions, and a gem-like internal glow that shifts colour with emotional state. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: metallic inclusions — gold, silver, and iron-ore veins — visible across the body surface, which no Terrae form possesses; a gem-like internal glow that shifts from deep amber (neutral) to red (threat) to near-violet (negotiations), unlike the fixed amber of Terrae orbitals; and articulated stone digits, but with a finer structure than the nobilis Terrae — individual finger-joints visible, capable of precise handling of objects. It moves with a deliberate stride with no floor-shaking — Dao are not tectonic, and their passage does not vibrate the ground the way Terrae does, and shows a shifting internal gem-glow, visible through the stone body, 30 m range. Even after dispersal it can be identified from precise stone-finger impressions where it has handled objects or steadied itself, and metallic trace-marks on surfaces it has gripped, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
c.eco: Dao persists in the Elemental Plane of Earth, deep gem-seams, and the material-world's deepest metamorphic intrusions; they visit the material world but do not form here. It does not breed; it forms where born in the Elemental Plane of Earth from deep planar pressure; they are not formed, they are generated, a distinction the Stonesong Heralds make carefully, and recurs whenever a Dao killed in the material world returns to the Elemental Plane; it does not re-form at the same site like a Terrae. It sustains its mass on planar elemental will, not tectonic pressure, drawing in metal and gem rather than rock rubble — a Dao incorporates precious metals and cut gems into its body, not fault debris. Range is bounded by material-world visits are bounded by planar contracts, not geology: outside that band the body does not fail like a Terrae; it can choose to withdraw to the Elemental Plane. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as not applicable; Dao are not nascent, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
c.beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Dao is roused by planar contract, summoning, or intrusion into their claimed gem-seam territories and otherwise lies a still dark-stone humanoid figure; at rest, the internal glow dims to a faint amber, distinguishable from a dead Terrae form by the metallic inclusions. It claims claimed gem-seams and deep planar territories and answers intrusion with negotiation before force; a Dao will make a contract offer before striking unless its territory is actively threatened. Toward grey-folk it is contractual and dangerous; survey crews report that the Forgemasters have a saying: 'a Dao offer is binding on both parties — read it before you touch the gem-seam'. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
c.traitsText: Stable characters of Dao: a body of dense layered dark stone with gold, silver, and iron-ore inclusions, animated by planar elemental will rather than tectonic pressure; immune to petrification, earth effects, and all forms that affect Terrae; it is not Terrae and does not share their failure modes; and a defining weakness — air and lightning, which disrupt the planar architecture; and a broken contract, which allows the opposing party to call for planar adjudication. Its senses run through the medium itself: tremor sensing far exceeding any Terrae form; metallic sensing that allows it to locate any significant ore or gem deposit within 1 km. It tolerates extreme heat, cold, and pressure but fails at planar contract breach and planar adjudication; direct lightning disruption. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, metallic inclusions, shifting gem-glow, and precise articulated digits, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
c.abilities: Abilities of Dao are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it can reshape stone with its articulated hands in seconds, opening or sealing passages at will; it can grant up to three wishes, but enforces precise literal interpretation — the Forgemasters call this 'the Dao reading'; it can travel through stone as through air, making walls and floors no obstacle; and it can call gem-seam material to itself, pulling ore and precious stone from surrounding rock. Each is a direct expression of planar elemental will operating through a dense multi-element stone body, so it cannot, for instance, be driven away by Terrae dispersal techniques; be bound by geology or fault pressure; or be harmed by the same methods that affect Terrae. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dao matters because Dao are believed responsible for several 'spontaneously sealed' gem-seam galleries in the lower Throat — passages that simply filled with stone overnight after a crew worked an unclaimed seam. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a planar-contact entry, not a field hazard; requires contract protocol if a crew intends to work near a claimed gem-seam, and its presence reshapes routes through the deep gem-seam territories, now flagged with a gold-chalk mark meaning 'possible planar claim'. The Forgemasters maintain a partial record of claimed gem-seam territories based on stories of sealed galleries. The record is unofficial and incomplete, because claiming a seam is a Dao prerogative that does not require grey-folk notification.

c.g: Dao Kin is the order-rank grouping of lower-tier Dao without noble-house standing, associated with base-metal ore-seams within the True Elementals. Its members share a Dao body with iron-ore-dominant inclusions, amber-only glow, and one-wish architecture, and divide below into family groups by iron ore type and seam specificity. The Dao Kin order covers Dao without noble-house standing — iron-ore rather than gold, one wish rather than three, and a more frequent presence in material-world ore-seams.
c.tax: Placed at order rank, Dao Kin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a lower-tier Dao with iron-ore-dominant inclusions and one-wish architecture. A new family belongs here only if it shows a Dao body with iron-ore-dominant inclusions.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by dark stone with rust-red seams and fixed amber glow. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — rust-red inclusions and amber-only non-shifting glow — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Dao Kin occupies iron and copper ore-seams and lower Elemental Plane. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at lower tier in the Elemental Plane and recur with returns to Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on lower-tier planar will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: ore-seam territorial; commercial rather than noble-house contractual. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: iron-inclusion body, one-wish, and stone-travel. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: stone-reshaping, one-wish granting, and passage-sealing. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dao Kin matters because the most commonly encountered Dao tier in working ore-seams. It lets survey reports sort ore-kin from noble-house Dao Lords by inclusion colour without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Dao Ore-Kin Houses is the family-rank grouping of lower-tier Dao of established ore-seam claim without noble-house status within the True Elementals. Its members share a Dao body with rust-red iron-ore inclusions and amber-only glow, and divide below into genus groups by iron ore type. Ore-Kin Houses are the working-tier Dao, encountered when crews drill iron seams.
c.tax: Placed at family rank, Dao Ore-Kin Houses is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: an iron-ore Dao body with established ore-seam claim. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a Dao with iron-ore inclusions and a material-world ore-seam claim.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by dark stone with rust-red seams. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — rust-red inclusions, amber-only glow, and rust-chalk territory marks — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Dao Ore-Kin Houses occupies iron and copper ore-seams. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at lower tier in the Elemental Plane and recur with returns to Elemental Plane, sustaining themselves on lower-tier planar will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: ore-seam territorial; enforces via passage-sealing. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: iron-inclusion body and one-wish architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: stone-reshaping and one-wish granting. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dao Ore-Kin Houses matters because the source of the Forgemasters' lease clause. It lets survey reports sort ore-kin from gem-seam noble Dao by seam type and inclusion colour without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Dao Ore-Kin is the genus-rank grouping of iron-ore-dominant lower-tier Dao of the Ore-Kin lineage within the True Elementals. Its members share a dark stone body with rust-red iron-ore inclusions, amber-only glow, and one-wish architecture, and divide below into species groups by iron-ore type and ore-seam specificity. The most commonly encountered Dao type in the working ore-seams of the lower tiers.
c.tax: Placed at genus rank, Dao Ore-Kin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a lower-tier Dao with iron-ore-dominant inclusions and one-wish capacity. A new species belongs here only if it shows an iron-ore Dao body with amber-only glow.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by dark stone with rust-red seams and fixed amber glow. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — rust-red inclusions and the absence of the violet negotiation-shift glow — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Dao Ore-Kin occupies iron and copper ore-seams. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at lower tier in the Elemental Plane and recur with returns to the Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on lower-tier planar will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: ore-seam territorial; demands contract acknowledgement. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: iron-inclusion body, one-wish, and stone-travel. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: stone-reshaping, one-wish granting, passage-sealing, and ore-calling. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dao Ore-Kin matters because the source of the Forgemasters' standard prior-claim lease clause. It lets survey reports sort ore-kin Dao from noble-house Dao Lords by inclusion colour and wish capacity without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Dao Ore-Kin (Geniis ferricula) is a lower-tier earth genie associated with iron and base-metal ore-seams, more frequently encountered in the material world than the noble-house Dao. Documented from iron and copper ore-seams of the deeper tiers and the lower Elemental Plane, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a dark stone body with prominent rust-red iron-ore inclusions and an amber-only internal glow that does not shift — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The Ore-Kin is to the noble Dao what a working Forgemaster is to a senior Herald — same lineage, different tier of standing, and a more hands-on relationship with material-world ore. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a collapse of iron-ore-veined dark stone that reconsolidates on the Elemental Plane rather than re-forming materially and no carcass.
c.tax: Dao Ore-Kin is placed at species rank beneath Dao Ore-Kin (Dao Kin order, Dao class). Diagnosis rests on rust-red iron-ore inclusions, amber-only non-shifting glow, and a one-wish architecture rather than three. It is separated from its sibling the Dao (noble-house) by its rust-red iron-ore inclusions and one-wish structure versus the noble Dao's gold inclusions and three-wish architecture; the two share the genus body-plan but never the gold-inclusion three-wish noble architecture. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: dark layered stone with iron-ore and occasional copper-ore inclusions, animated by lower-tier planar elemental will.
c.ap: Dao Ore-Kin stands 2.5–4 m, slightly smaller than the noble-house Dao. Its silhouette is a stone humanoid with rust-red seams, an amber-only gem-glow, and slightly less precise digit articulation than the noble form. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: rust-red iron-ore inclusions clearly visible across the body — the definitive distinguishing mark from the noble Dao's gold veins; an amber-only internal glow that does not shift colour regardless of emotional state — the absence of the violet negotiation-glow is significant; and less precisely articulated digits than the noble Dao — joints visible but less fine-grained, capable of handling but less delicate than noble-house manipulation. It moves with a heavier stride than the noble Dao, producing slight floor vibration, closer to a large Terrae form's footfall though still non-tectonic, and shows amber-only, fixed, 20 m range. Even after dispersal it can be identified from iron-ore dust shed along its path and hand-impressions with a rust-red tinge, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
c.eco: Dao Ore-Kin persists in iron and copper ore-seams of the deeper tiers and the lower Elemental Plane of Earth, where it guards ore-body territories rather than gem-seam territories. It does not breed; it forms where generated at lower tier in the Elemental Plane; it does not form from fault pressure any more than the noble Dao does, and recurs whenever returns to the Elemental Plane when killed; its ore-seam claim persists, enforced by other Ore-Kin. It sustains its mass on lower-tier planar elemental will, drawing in iron ore and base metals rather than precious metals and gems. Range is bounded by material-world visits bounded by lower-tier planar contract: outside that band the body withdraws to the Elemental Plane; does not fail geologically. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as not applicable, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
c.beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Dao Ore-Kin is roused by intrusion into its ore-seam territory or contact with base-metal equipment it claims as part of its seam and otherwise lies a still dark-stone figure with rust-red seams; at rest, the amber glow dims but does not shift. It claims iron and copper ore-seams in the deep tiers and answers intrusion with demands contract acknowledgement before granting passage; will enforce territory by sealing ore-access passages rather than direct combat if possible. Toward grey-folk it is commercial and territorial rather than contractual-noble; survey crews report that the Forgemasters' iron-seam leases include a standard clause acknowledging that the ore-body may have a prior planar claim; this clause has no legal force but is understood to avoid Ore-Kin territorial enforcement. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
c.traitsText: Stable characters of Dao Ore-Kin: a body of dark layered stone with iron-ore and occasional copper-ore inclusions, animated by lower-tier planar elemental will; immune to earth effects and petrification; iron-ore body resists lightning better than the noble Dao's stone; and a defining weakness — air and fire, which disrupt the lower-tier planar architecture more easily than noble-tier; a broken ore-claim acknowledgement. Its senses run through the medium itself: metallic sensing at 500 m for iron and base metals; tremor sensing less refined than the noble Dao. It tolerates heat, cold, and pressure but fails at air and fire disruption and contract breach. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, rust-red iron-ore inclusions, amber-only glow, and one-wish capacity, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
c.abilities: Abilities of Dao Ore-Kin are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it can grant one wish, enforcing precise literal interpretation; it can seal ore-access passages by reshaping surrounding stone; it can travel through stone as through air; and it can call iron-ore deposits to itself, accelerating natural veining. Each is a direct expression of lower-tier planar elemental will in an iron-ore-dominant dark stone body, so it cannot, for instance, be confused with a Terrae by the rust-red inclusions and non-tectonic gait; shift its glow colour; grant more than one wish. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dao Ore-Kin matters because the most commonly encountered Dao type in the working tiers, and the source of the Forgemasters' standard lease clause acknowledging prior planar claims. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a planar-contact entry; not a field hazard but a territory-negotiation context, and its presence reshapes routes through the deep iron and copper ore-seams, now flagged with a rust-chalk mark meaning 'possible lower-tier planar claim'. Forgemaster oral tradition holds that a seam that yields unusually well in the first season, then yields nothing thereafter, has been 'recognised' by an Ore-Kin that has called the best ore to itself.

c.g: Efreeti is the class-rank grouping of fire-element genie nobles: planar entities of the Elemental Plane of Fire within the True Elementals. Its members share a brass exterior over a living fire interior, wish-granting architecture, and planar origin in the Elemental Plane of Fire; no char-ring footprint distinguishes them from all Ignis forms, and divide below into order groups by planar tier and trade specialisation. The Efreeti class is the formal placement for fire-element genie nobles. They are citizens of the City of Brass, not geological formations.
c.tax: Placed at class rank, Efreeti is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin brass-exterior fire-interior humanoid with wish-granting architecture; formed in the Elemental Plane, not by vent pressure. A new order belongs here only if it shows a planar-origin entity with brass exterior, fire interior, and no char-ring footprint.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a brass-toned humanoid with joint-flame glow and no floor-charring. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — no char-ring, metallic grip-marks, and sulphur residue — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Efreeti occupies the City of Brass and volcanic material-world zones. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane of Fire and recur with returns to the Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on planar fire-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: commercial, territorial, and wish-granting under precise terms. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: brass exterior, fire interior, no char-ring, wish-granting, and fire-travel. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each order and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: internal-flame exposure, fire-travel, wish-granting, and metal-calling. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Efreeti matters because their transit signs in the Gorge would indicate a planar trade route of major significance. It lets survey reports sort Efreeti from Ignis forms and from Dao, Djinn, and Marid by element without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Efreeti Lords is the order-rank grouping of high-tier Efreeti of confirmed City of Brass noble standing within the True Elementals. Its members share a brass exterior with gold-inlay markings, fire interior with white-hot core, and three-wish architecture, and divide below into family groups by City of Brass noble house affiliation. Efreeti Lords carry formal City of Brass noble titles. Their gold-inlay markings are visible.
c.tax: Placed at order rank, Efreeti Lords is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a City-of-Brass-noble-tier Efreeti with gold-inlay exterior markings. A new family belongs here only if it shows an Efreeti with confirmed City of Brass noble affiliation.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by brass-toned humanoid with gold-inlay markings and a brighter joint-flame glow. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — gold-inlay markings on the brass exterior — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Efreeti Lords occupies City of Brass and deep volcanic zones. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at noble tier in the Elemental Plane of Fire and recur with returns to Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on noble-tier planar fire-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: formal and contractual; three-wish capacity. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: gold-inlay brass exterior, white-hot fire interior, and three-wish architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: fire-travel, three-wish granting, and internal-flame full exposure. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Efreeti Lords matters because City of Brass noble rank is a formal planar designation. It lets survey reports sort noble-house Efreeti from lesser trade-kin without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: City of Brass Noble Houses is the family-rank grouping of Efreeti of established City of Brass noble-house affiliation within the True Elementals. Its members share a brass-exterior with house-specific gold-inlay pattern, and divide below into genus groups by noble-house affiliation. Noble house affiliation is heritable and indicated by gold-inlay pattern.
c.tax: Placed at family rank, City of Brass Noble Houses is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a noble-house Efreeti with house-specific gold-inlay. A new genus belongs here only if it shows an Efreeti with confirmed noble-house gold-inlay.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by brass-toned humanoid with distinctive gold-inlay house markings. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — house-specific gold-inlay pattern — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: City of Brass Noble Houses occupies City of Brass and noble-house volcanic territories. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at noble-house tier and recur with returns to Elemental Plane, sustaining themselves on noble-tier planar will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: formal three-wish granting and house-territory enforcement. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: gold-inlay noble exterior and three-wish architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: three-wish granting and fire-travel. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, City of Brass Noble Houses matters because the most dangerous Efreeti tier if encountered. It lets survey reports sort noble-house from trade-kin by gold-inlay without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Efreeti is the genus-rank grouping of fire-element genie nobles of the Efreeti lineage within the True Elementals. Its members share a brass exterior over a living fire interior, no char-ring footprint, and planar origin in the Elemental Plane of Fire, and divide below into species groups by planar standing and trade specialisation. The Efreeti genus covers all Efreeti known from the Throat's survey tradition, which is essentially none — but the diagnostic signs are recorded for completeness.
c.tax: Placed at genus rank, Efreeti is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin brass-exterior fire-interior humanoid with no char-ring footprint. A new species belongs here only if it shows a planar-origin entity with brass exterior, fire interior, and wish-granting architecture.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a brass-toned humanoid with internal-flame joint-glow and no floor-charring. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — no char-ring, metallic grip-marks, and sulphur residue — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Efreeti occupies the City of Brass and volcanic material-world zones. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane of Fire and recur with returns to the Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on planar fire-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: commercial and territorial; transitions from non-charring to lethal-heat when threatened. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: brass exterior, fire interior, no char-ring, and wish-granting architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: internal-flame exposure, fire-travel, wish-granting, and metal-calling. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Efreeti matters because their transit signs in the deep Gorge would indicate a planar trade route. It lets survey reports sort Efreeti from Ignis forms by the definitive no-char-ring field sign without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Efreeti (Geniis ignis) is a fire-element planar noble: a powerful intelligence native to the City of Brass on the Elemental Plane of Fire, taking the form of a towering brass-skinned humanoid wreathed in internal flame. Documented from the City of Brass on the Elemental Plane of Fire and volcanic deep-zones of the material world, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a brass-and-flame humanoid body: a hard exterior of enchanted brass-coloured elemental material over a living flame interior — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The Efreeti is not a large Ignis form with intelligence — its brass exterior, fire-immunity to suppression, and planar origin separate it entirely from material-world fire elementals. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a slag-like cooling of the brass exterior and a final hot exhalation, with no char-ring — Efreeti do not leave char-rings on the floor and no carcass.
c.tax: Efreeti is placed at species rank beneath Efreeti (Efreeti Lords order, Efreeti class). Diagnosis rests on a brass-exterior fire-interior humanoid body with no char-ring footprint, a planar origin, and a distinctive smell of hot metal and sulphur. It is separated from its sibling Ignis elementalis nobilis by its brass exterior, planar origin, and city-citizenship versus the nobilis Ignis's three-layer differentiated pure-flame body formed in material metamorphic terrain; the two share the genus body-plan but never the material-world differentiated flame formation. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: an enchanted brass-coloured elemental exterior over a living fire interior, animated by planar elemental will in the Elemental Plane of Fire.
c.ap: Efreeti stands 4–6 m, typically with a wider and more defined humanoid structure than Ignis major. Its silhouette is a powerfully built brass-toned humanoid wreathed in internal flame visible through joints, eyes, and mouth. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a brass-coloured exterior that does not char the floor beneath it — the definitive sign distinguishing Efreeti from all Ignis forms, which always leave char-rings; flame visible through joints and eye-sockets, flickering independently of the exterior — the internal flame is a separate structure, not a surface burn; and a smell of hot metal and volcanic sulphur, distinct from the wood-gas smell of fuel-vent Ignis forms. It moves with a slow powerful stride with no floor-charring and minimal heat radiation at 2 m, until threatened, at which point the internal flame expands through the exterior, and shows a warm orange-red visible through joints and eyes at 50 m; hot-metal orange ambient glow when the internal flame expands. Even after dispersal it can be identified from no char-ring; instead, metallic contact-marks where it has gripped stone and a sulphur residue where it stood, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
c.eco: Efreeti persists in the City of Brass and the material world's volcanic deep-zones and lava-tube systems; they are traders, slavers, and territory-holders rather than geological formations. It does not breed; it forms where born in the Elemental Plane of Fire; not formed from vent pressure or lava, and recurs whenever returns to the Elemental Plane of Fire when killed in the material world; does not re-form materially. It sustains its mass on planar fire-will, drawing in metal and magical items rather than fuel gas — Efreeti incorporate enchanted metals into their exterior structure. Range is bounded by material-world visits bounded by planar contract or summoning: outside that band the body withdraws to the Elemental Plane; does not fail like an Ignis. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as not applicable, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
c.beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Efreeti is roused by planar contract, summoning, or intrusion into their lava-zone territories and otherwise lies a still brass-toned humanoid with faintly glowing joints — the only fire entity that can be in a passage without charring the floor. It claims lava-zone trade routes and planar merchant territories and answers intrusion with contract offer or demand before force; when force is used, the brass exterior opens and the full internal flame is exposed — a transition from 10 m of non-charring to 10 m of killing heat. Toward grey-folk it is commercial and dangerous; survey crews report that the Forgemasters have no active Efreeti protocol, but the Stonesong Heralds record that a passage with metallic grip-marks and sulphur residue but no char-ring is an Efreeti transit route — an extraordinarily significant finding in the Gorge. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
c.traitsText: Stable characters of Efreeti: a body of an enchanted brass-coloured elemental exterior over a living fire interior, animated by planar elemental will in the Elemental Plane of Fire; immune to fire and heat; the brass exterior also provides resistance to physical damage that Ignis forms lack; and a defining weakness — water and cold, which crack the brass exterior and suppress the internal flame; and a broken trade contract. Its senses run through the medium itself: heat and metal sensing; fire-gradient sensing that locates all Ignis forms within 2 km. It tolerates extreme heat, volcanic gas, and darkness but fails at water flooding and cold sustained long enough to crack the brass exterior. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, brass exterior, no char-ring footprint, and internal-flame joint-glow, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
c.abilities: Abilities of Efreeti are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it can expose its internal flame by opening the brass exterior — a dramatic transition from non-charring to lethal heat; it can grant up to three wishes, with a known preference for fire-element outcomes in the literal interpretation; it can travel through fire as through air, making any flame an exit or entrance; and it can call metal objects to itself, incorporating enchanted items into its exterior. Each is a direct expression of planar fire-will in a brass-exterior-over-living-flame planar body, so it cannot, for instance, be identified by char-ring survey (it leaves none); be affected by Ignis suppression techniques; or be driven away by fuel-starvation. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
c.bg: In the Throat, Efreeti matters because metallic grip-marks with sulphur residue and no char-ring is the definitive Efreeti transit sign — if found in the Gorge's lava-floor galleries, it would indicate a planar trade route of significant concern to the Forgemasters. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a planar-contact entry; not an active Gorge hazard; requires trade-contract protocol if encountered, and its presence reshapes routes through the deep lava-floor galleries, flagged with a brass-chalk mark meaning 'possible Efreeti transit route'. The Heralds' notation for a confirmed Efreeti passage is a brass-chalk horizontal line — the trade-route mark. It has never been placed inside the active Gorge, only in survey notes from the deeper speculative tiers.

c.g: Efreeti Trade-Kin is the order-rank grouping of lower-tier Efreeti of merchant standing without formal noble-house affiliation within the True Elementals. Its members share a brass exterior without gold inlay, fire interior, and one-wish architecture, and divide below into family groups by trade specialisation and material-world territory type. Trade-Kin are the merchant-class Efreeti, more frequently present in material-world volcanic zones on trade business.
c.tax: Placed at order rank, Efreeti Trade-Kin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a merchant-tier Efreeti without noble-house gold inlay. A new family belongs here only if it shows an Efreeti with plain brass exterior and one-wish architecture.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by plain brass-toned humanoid without gold markings. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — no char-ring, metallic grip-marks, and sulphur residue — but no gold inlay on the exterior — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Efreeti Trade-Kin occupies material-world volcanic trade zones. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at merchant tier in the Elemental Plane and recur with returns to Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on merchant-tier planar fire-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: commercial and transactional; one-wish capacity. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: plain brass exterior and one-wish architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: one-wish granting and fire-travel. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Efreeti Trade-Kin matters because the most frequently encountered Efreeti tier in material-world volcanic zones. It lets survey reports sort trade-kin from noble-house Efreeti by absence of gold inlay without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Efreeti Merchant Houses is the family-rank grouping of merchant-tier Efreeti of established trade-house affiliation within the True Elementals. Its members share a plain brass exterior and one-wish architecture, and divide below into genus groups by trade-house affiliation and material-world zone. Merchant houses are the working-tier Efreeti, handling material-world trade.
c.tax: Placed at family rank, Efreeti Merchant Houses is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a merchant-house Efreeti with plain brass exterior. A new genus belongs here only if it shows an Efreeti with plain brass exterior and trade-house affiliation.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by plain brass humanoid with joint-flame glow. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — no char-ring and metallic grip-marks without gold inlay — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Efreeti Merchant Houses occupies material-world volcanic trade zones. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at merchant tier and recur with returns to Elemental Plane, sustaining themselves on merchant-tier planar will.

c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: one-wish granting and fire-travel. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Efreeti Merchant Houses matters because the most common Efreeti tier in working volcanic zones. It lets survey reports sort merchant-tier from noble-house by absence of gold inlay without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Djinn is the class-rank grouping of air-element genie nobles: planar entities of the Elemental Plane of Air within the True Elementals. Its members share a translucent ordered-air humanoid body, surrounding mini-weather, and wish-granting architecture from the Jann stem, and divide below into order groups by planar tier and wind-specialisation. The Djinn class covers air-element genie nobles. Not geological, not meteorological — planar.
c.tax: Placed at class rank, Djinn is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin translucent air-humanoid with internal directional current and wish-granting architecture. A new order belongs here only if it shows a planar-origin translucent air entity with directional current and wish capacity.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a translucent tall humanoid distortion with surrounding local weather. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — wind-direction anomaly against the geological draught — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Djinn occupies the Elemental Plane of Air and storm-shaft convergences. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane and recur with returns to Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on planar air-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: capricious, weather-conditional, and wish-granting. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: translucent air-body, directional current, mini-weather, and wish-granting. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each order and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: storm-creation, wind-travel, wish-granting, and planar whirlwind. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Djinn matters because wind-direction anomalies in still shafts are significant intelligence findings. It lets survey reports sort Djinn from Aeolian elementals and from Dao, Efreeti, and Marid without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Djinn Lords is the order-rank grouping of high-tier Djinn of confirmed planar-noble standing within the True Elementals. Its members share a luminous internal current and three-wish architecture, and divide below into family groups by noble house and storm-specialisation. Djinn Lords carry visible internal luminosity marking their noble standing.
c.tax: Placed at order rank, Djinn Lords is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a noble-tier Djinn with luminous internal current. A new family belongs here only if it shows a Djinn with confirmed noble standing and three-wish capacity.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a luminous translucent air-humanoid with bright internal current. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — bright internal luminosity and active lightning in surrounding weather — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Djinn Lords occupies Elemental Plane noble territories. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at noble tier and recur with returns to Elemental Plane, sustaining themselves on noble-tier planar air-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: noble-formal; weather-warning and three-wish. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.

c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: three-wish granting, storm creation, and wind-travel. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Djinn Lords matters because the most powerful Djinn tier for wish-granting capacity. It lets survey reports sort noble Djinn from trade-kin by internal luminosity without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Djinn Noble Currents is the family-rank grouping of noble-house Djinn of established planar lineage within the True Elementals. Its members share a noble-house air-body with house-specific current pattern, and divide below into genus groups by noble house affiliation. Noble current pattern is a heritable planar trait.
c.tax: Placed at family rank, Djinn Noble Currents is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a noble-house Djinn with house-specific current pattern. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a Djinn with confirmed noble-house current pattern.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a luminous translucent humanoid with distinctive current pattern. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — house-specific current pattern and active lightning — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Djinn Noble Currents occupies noble-house planar territories. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at noble-house tier and recur with returns to Elemental Plane, sustaining themselves on noble planar will.
