c.bg: In the Throat, Djinn Noble Currents matters because noble-house wind-territory enforcement. It lets survey reports sort noble-current from trade-current Djinn without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Djinn is the genus-rank grouping of air-element genie nobles of the Djinn lineage within the True Elementals. Its members share a translucent air-humanoid body with a directional internal current and a surrounding mini-weather event, and divide below into species groups by planar standing and wish-granting capacity. The Djinn genus covers all Djinn lineage, which in Throat survey tradition is defined solely by the wind-direction anomaly field sign.
c.tax: Placed at genus rank, Djinn is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin translucent air-humanoid with directional internal current and weather-event generation. A new species belongs here only if it shows a translucent air-body with internal directional current and wish-granting architecture.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a translucent tall humanoid distortion in the air with a surrounding local weather. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — wind-direction against the geological draught and localised cloud formation — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Djinn occupies the Elemental Plane of Air and storm-shaft convergences. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane of Air and recur with returns to Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on planar air-will.

c.g: Djinn (Geniis aeris) is an air-element planar noble: a tall, cloud-and-wind-bodied intelligence native to the Elemental Plane of Air, appearing as a turbaned storm-figure or as a column of translucent moving air. Documented from the Elemental Plane of Air and storm-shaft convergences of the upper material world, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a translucent air-and-storm humanoid body with a visible internal current and a surrounding mini-weather event — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The Djinn is not a large Whirler or Stormling — its translucent air-body produces no shaft-debris, its internal current is directional rather than random, and its planar origin gives it wish-granting architecture. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a sudden dispersal of wind and a final cool gust, no scorch and no debris pile and no carcass.
c.tax: Djinn is placed at species rank beneath Djinn (Djinn Lords order, Djinn class). Diagnosis rests on a translucent air-humanoid with a visible internal directional current, a surrounding mini-weather event, and no debris trail. It is separated from its sibling Gust Whirler by its translucent ordered air-body with internal directional current versus the Whirler's random spinning debris-cloud body; the two share the genus body-plan but never the random spinning debris-cloud. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: ordered air-current in a translucent humanoid form, animated by planar air-will.
c.ap: Djinn stands 5–7 m, the tallest of the genie classes typically. Its silhouette is a translucent humanoid of moving air with a visible internal directional current and a surrounding local weather event — clouds, brief rain, or lightning depending on emotional state. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a visible internal directional current — air moving in a consistent laminar direction through the body, unlike the random spin of Aeolian elementals; a surrounding mini-weather event: brief cloud formation at 10 m range when calm, lightning within the cloud when threatened; and translucency — the Djinn body is visible as a distortion and a moving air-current rather than an opaque body; stone behind it is visible through it. It moves with a floating stride that produces a localised wind around it at 5 m, but no significant floor vibration, and shows a soft luminescence inside the air-body, brighter during lightning-states; visible 30 m. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a trail of unusual wind direction and brief moisture marks on walls from the surrounding weather event, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
c.eco: Djinn persists in the Elemental Plane of Air and material-world storm-shaft convergences, where planar air pressure meets geological shaft geometry. It does not breed; it forms where born in the Elemental Plane of Air; not formed from shaft draught, and recurs whenever returns to the Elemental Plane when killed; does not re-form in the material shaft. It sustains its mass on planar air-will, drawing in wind and weather rather than fuel gas. Range is bounded by material-world visits bounded by planar contract: outside that band the body withdraws to the Elemental Plane. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as not applicable, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
c.beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Djinn is roused by planar contract or summoning; wind-direction manipulation near their claimed shaft territories and otherwise lies a tall translucent distortion in the air of a shaft, with a barely perceptible internal current. It claims claimed storm-shaft routes and wind-corridor trade paths and answers intrusion with a weather-event warning — cloud formation and lightning — before physical engagement; the Djinn prefer to intimidate with weather before using the body directly. Toward grey-folk it is capricious and weather-conditional; survey crews report that Mist-Touched scouts note a localised wind-change in a still shaft as the primary Djinn presence indicator — a change of direction against the geological draught is significant. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
c.traitsText: Stable characters of Djinn: a body of ordered air-current in a translucent humanoid form, animated by planar air-will; immune to air effects and lightning; the translucent body also provides resistance to physical attack (most blows pass through); and a defining weakness — earth and water containment, which ground and suppress the air-body. Its senses run through the medium itself: wind-current sensing at vast range; the Djinn can detect air-pressure changes across an entire geological formation. It tolerates cold, lightning, and high altitude but fails at earth and water containment sealing the air-body from its elemental source. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, translucent air-body, internal directional current, and surrounding mini-weather event, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
c.abilities: Abilities of Djinn are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it can create a localised storm around itself with wind, rain, and lightning; it can grant up to three wishes with a preference for air-and-travel outcomes; it can travel through air at enormous speed, moving across the Gorge in seconds; and it can become a whirlwind of planar intensity, far exceeding any Aeolian elemental. Each is a direct expression of planar air-will in a translucent ordered-air humanoid form, so it cannot, for instance, be confused with an Aeolian by the directional internal current; be affected by Aeolian suppression; or be contained by enclosure alone without earth and water. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
c.bg: In the Throat, Djinn matters because a wind-direction anomaly in a still shaft — against the geological draught — is the primary field indicator of Djinn presence, and Mist-Touched scouts are trained to report it rather than investigate independently. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a planar-contact entry; not an active Gorge hazard but a significant intelligence finding if the wind-anomaly sign is located, and its presence reshapes routes through upper shaft passages with anomalous wind-direction, flagged with a cloud-chalk mark. Mist-Touched oral tradition includes a saying: 'the wind that doesn't know which way the Gorge runs is a wind that answers to a different map.'

c.g: Djinn Trade-Winds is the order-rank grouping of lower-tier Djinn of merchant standing without formal noble affiliation within the True Elementals. Its members share a translucent air-body with dim internal current and one-wish architecture, and divide below into family groups by trade route specialisation. Trade-Wind Djinn are the commercial tier, handling material-world transit business.
c.tax: Placed at order rank, Djinn Trade-Winds is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a merchant-tier Djinn with dim internal current and one-wish capacity. A new family belongs here only if it shows a Djinn with dim internal current and trade-wind affiliation.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a translucent air-humanoid with dim current, no active lightning at rest. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — wind-direction anomaly and localised cloud without lightning at rest — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Djinn Trade-Winds occupies material-world trade shaft routes. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at merchant tier in the Elemental Plane and recur with returns to Elemental Plane, sustaining themselves on merchant-tier planar air-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: commercial and one-wish; weather-warning still applies. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: translucent air-body and one-wish architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: one-wish granting and wind-travel. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Djinn Trade-Winds matters because the most commonly encountered Djinn tier in material-world shaft routes. It lets survey reports sort trade-wind from noble Djinn by dim internal current without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Trade-Wind Djinn Houses is the family-rank grouping of merchant-tier Djinn of established trade-house affiliation within the True Elementals. Its members share a translucent air-body with dim current and one-wish capacity, and divide below into genus groups by trade-house specialisation. Trade-house Djinn handle material-world shaft-route business.

Its members share a translucent air-humanoid body with a directional internal current and a surrounding mini-weather event, and divide below into species groups by planar standing and wish-granting capacity. The Djinn genus covers all Djinn lineage, which in Throat survey tradition is defined solely by the wind-direction anomaly field sign.
c.tax: Placed at genus rank, Djinn is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin translucent air-humanoid with directional internal current and weather-event generation. A new species belongs here only if it shows a translucent air-body with internal directional current and wish-granting architecture.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a translucent tall humanoid distortion in the air with a surrounding local weather. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — wind-direction against the geological draught and localised cloud formation — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Djinn occupies the Elemental Plane of Air and storm-shaft convergences. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane of Air and recur with returns to Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on planar air-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: capricious and weather-conditional; weather-warning before force. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.

c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: storm-creation, wind-travel, wish-granting, and planar whirlwind. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Djinn matters because wind anomalies in still shafts are the only practical field indicator in the Gorge. It lets survey reports sort Djinn from Aeolian elementals by directional internal current vs random spin without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Djinn (Geniis aeris) is an air-element planar noble: a tall, cloud-and-wind-bodied intelligence native to the Elemental Plane of Air, appearing as a turbaned storm-figure or as a column of translucent moving air. Documented from the Elemental Plane of Air and storm-shaft convergences of the upper material world, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a translucent air-and-storm humanoid body with a visible internal current and a surrounding mini-weather event — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The Djinn is not a large Whirler or Stormling — its translucent air-body produces no shaft-debris, its internal current is directional rather than random, and its planar origin gives it wish-granting architecture. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a sudden dispersal of wind and a final cool gust, no scorch and no debris pile and no carcass.
c.tax: Djinn is placed at species rank beneath Djinn (Djinn Lords order, Djinn class). Diagnosis rests on a translucent air-humanoid with a visible internal directional current, a surrounding mini-weather event, and no debris trail. It is separated from its sibling Gust Whirler by its translucent ordered air-body with internal directional current versus the Whirler's random spinning debris-cloud body; the two share the genus body-plan but never the random spinning debris-cloud. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: ordered air-current in a translucent humanoid form, animated by planar air-will.

c.g: Marid is the class-rank grouping of water-element genie nobles: planar entities of the Elemental Plane of Water within the True Elementals. Its members share a living-ocean water body with bioluminescent internal currents, wish-granting architecture, and planar origin, and divide below into order groups by planar tier and ocean-territory specialisation. The Marid class covers water-element genie nobles. They are ocean-depth planar entities, not geological water formations.
c.tax: Placed at class rank, Marid is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin ocean-water humanoid with bioluminescent currents and wish-granting architecture. A new order belongs here only if it shows a planar-origin ocean-water entity with bioluminescent currents and wish capacity.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a vast blue-green luminescent water humanoid. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — bioluminescent blue-green internal currents and deep-ocean smell — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Marid occupies the Elemental Plane of Water and deep underground seas and oceans. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane of Water and recur with returns to Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on planar water-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: distant and powerful; tidal-event warning before force. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.

c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: tidal events, water-travel, wish-granting, and passage-flooding. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Marid matters because bioluminescent pools in the deep tiers would indicate planar water-territory claims. It lets survey reports sort Marid from Fluvial/Glacial elementals and from Dao, Efreeti, and Djinn without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Marid Lords is the order-rank grouping of high-tier Marid of confirmed planar-noble standing within the True Elementals. Its members share an ocean-water body with bright bioluminescent currents and three-wish architecture, and divide below into family groups by noble house and ocean-territory type. Marid Lords carry intensely bright bioluminescent currents marking their noble standing.
c.tax: Placed at order rank, Marid Lords is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a noble-tier Marid with bright bioluminescent currents. A new family belongs here only if it shows a Marid with confirmed noble standing and three-wish capacity.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a vast intensely luminescent blue-green water humanoid. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — intense bioluminescent currents visible at 100 m — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Marid Lords occupies Elemental Plane noble territories and claimed deep seas. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at noble tier and recur with returns to Elemental Plane, sustaining themselves on noble-tier planar water-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: powerful and formal; three-wish capacity. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.

c.bg: In the Throat, Marid Lords matters because the most powerful Marid tier; claimed deep seas are defended vigorously. It lets survey reports sort noble Marid from trade-kin by luminescence intensity without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Marid Noble Depths is the family-rank grouping of Marid of established noble-house depth territory within the True Elementals. Its members share a noble-house ocean-body with house-specific bioluminescent pattern, and divide below into genus groups by noble house and depth territory. Noble depth territory is heritable and marked by luminescent pattern.
c.tax: Placed at family rank, Marid Noble Depths is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a noble-house Marid with house-specific luminescent pattern. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a Marid with confirmed noble-house affiliation.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a vast luminescent water humanoid with distinctive pattern. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — house-specific bioluminescent pattern and deep-ocean smell — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Marid Noble Depths occupies noble-house deep-sea territories. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at noble-house tier and recur with returns to Elemental Plane, sustaining themselves on noble planar will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: formal three-wish and depth-territory enforcement. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.

c.bg: In the Throat, Marid Noble Depths matters because noble-depth territory enforcement and claimed underground seas. It lets survey reports sort noble-depth from trade-depth Marid by luminescence without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Marid is the genus-rank grouping of water-element genie nobles of the Marid lineage within the True Elementals. Its members share a living-ocean water body with bioluminescent internal currents and deep-ocean colouration, and divide below into species groups by planar standing and ocean-territory type. The Marid genus covers all Marid lineage, defined by bioluminescent internal currents and ocean-blue colouration.
c.tax: Placed at genus rank, Marid is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin living-ocean humanoid with bioluminescent internal currents. A new species belongs here only if it shows an ocean-water humanoid with bioluminescent currents and wish-granting architecture.

c.g: Marid (Geniis aquae) is a water-element planar noble: a vast, sea-deep intelligence native to the Elemental Plane of Water, appearing as a towering humanoid of living ocean — a moving sea-column with a face. Documented from the Elemental Plane of Water and the deepest lake and ocean systems of the material world, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a humanoid body of living ocean water — dark blue-green, self-cohering, with internal bioluminescent currents — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The Marid is not a large Surger or Spume — its ocean-depth internal currents, bioluminescent glow, and planar origin place it in a distinct genie lineage. It does not form from sump pressure; it arrives from the deep. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a controlled disbanding into the surrounding water, leaving a blue-green bioluminescent glow that fades over hours and no carcass.
c.tax: Marid is placed at species rank beneath Marid (Marid Lords order, Marid class). Diagnosis rests on a living-ocean humanoid body with internal bioluminescent currents and ocean-depth colouration distinguishing it from all material-world water elementals. It is separated from its sibling Sump Surger by its ocean-water bioluminescent self-cohering body with planar origin versus the Surger's sump-water dark still-column bound to a specific pool; the two share the genus body-plan but never the sump-bound still-water formation. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: living ocean water animated by planar water-will, self-cohering at height without any pool to feed from.
c.ap: Marid stands 6–10 m, the largest of the genie classes typically. Its silhouette is a vast towering humanoid of blue-green ocean water with internal bioluminescent currents visible through the body. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: internal bioluminescent currents visible through the body — moving blue-green light inside a water column — which no material-world water elemental possesses; ocean-blue-green colouration rather than the dark sump-water or white aerated foam of material-world water elementals; and a smell of deep ocean — brine, depth-mineral, and ozone — rather than the mineral or volcanic smell of sump water. It moves with a slow towering stride that produces waves at 10 m, but without the floor-pressure of a Terrae form, and shows bioluminescent blue-green internal glow, 80 m range in darkness. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a trail of unusual mineral-blue water and bioluminescent marks on surrounding surfaces, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
c.eco: Marid persists in the Elemental Plane of Water, deep ocean systems, and the material world's deepest lake formations; they visit material lakes and underground seas, not surface sumps. It does not breed; it forms where born in the Elemental Plane of Water; not formed from sump pressure, and recurs whenever returns to the Elemental Plane when killed; does not re-form in a material pool. It sustains its mass on planar water-will, drawing in ocean minerals and elemental water rather than sump-water. Range is bounded by material visits bounded by planar contract: outside that band the body withdraws to the Elemental Plane. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as not applicable, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
c.beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Marid is roused by planar contract, summoning, or intrusion into their claimed underwater territories and otherwise lies a still blue-green water-column in a deep pool or underground sea, with a faint bioluminescent glow. It claims claimed underwater territories in deep material lakes and oceans and answers intrusion with tidal event warning — a sudden rise of water and localised current — before physical engagement. Toward grey-folk it is distant and powerful; survey crews report that Stillwardens have a saying: 'a bioluminescent pool in the deep tiers is either a Marid's resting ground or a sign of something worse'. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
c.traitsText: Stable characters of Marid: a body of living ocean water animated by planar water-will, self-cohering at height without any pool to feed from; immune to water and cold; the ocean-water body also resists freezing far more effectively than any Glacial elemental; and a defining weakness — lightning and fire, which disrupt the planar water architecture from the inside; and a broken territorial agreement. Its senses run through the medium itself: water-current sensing at oceanic range; the Marid can detect any significant water-body or creature movement within its claimed sea territory. It tolerates extreme cold, deep pressure, and darkness but fails at lightning and fire sustained at the body's interior. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, bioluminescent internal currents, ocean-blue colouration, and deep-ocean smell, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
c.abilities: Abilities of Marid are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it can create a tidal event around itself — a localised flood or current-wave extending 20 m; it can grant up to three wishes with a preference for water and sea-travel outcomes; it can travel through water at oceanic speed, covering the Gorge's entire water system in seconds; and it can breathe water into enclosed spaces, flooding passages in minutes. Each is a direct expression of planar water-will in a vast self-cohering ocean-water humanoid, so it cannot, for instance, be confused with a Surger by the bioluminescent currents; be affected by material-water elemental suppression; or be dried out like a Glacial. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
c.bg: In the Throat, Marid matters because a bioluminescent blue-green glow in a deep underground lake or sea is the definitive Marid presence indicator — if found in the Gorge, it would indicate a planar claim on the deep water system. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a planar-contact entry; not an active Gorge hazard; a bioluminescent pool is flagged with a sea-chalk mark, and its presence reshapes routes through deep underground lakes and seas, flagged with a blue-green chalk mark meaning 'possible Marid territory'. Stillwarden oral tradition holds that the underground sea's depth cannot be measured with a standard cord — a line that goes slack without touching bottom has reached a Marid's territory, not the actual floor.

c.g: Marid Trade-Currents is the order-rank grouping of lower-tier Marid of merchant standing within the True Elementals. Trade-Current Marid handle material-world deep-water trade business.

c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: commercial and one-wish; tidal warning still applies. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: dim ocean-water body and one-wish architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: one-wish granting and water-travel. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Marid Trade-Currents matters because the most frequently encountered Marid tier in deep material lakes. It lets survey reports sort trade-tier from noble Marid by luminescence intensity without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Trade-Current Marid Houses is the family-rank grouping of merchant-tier Marid of trade-house affiliation within the True Elementals. Its members share a dim bioluminescent ocean-water body and one-wish capacity, and divide below into genus groups by trade-house and current specialisation. The working-tier Marid, handling material-world deep-lake trade.
c.tax: Placed at family rank, Trade-Current Marid Houses is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a merchant-tier Marid with dim luminescence. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a Marid with dim bioluminescent currents and trade affiliation.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a large blue-green water humanoid with minimal luminescence. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — dim currents and deep-ocean smell — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Trade-Current Marid Houses occupies deep material-world lake trade routes. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated at merchant tier and recur with returns to Elemental Plane, sustaining themselves on merchant-tier planar will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: commercial one-wish and water-transit. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: dim ocean-water body and one-wish architecture. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: one-wish granting and water-travel. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Trade-Current Marid Houses matters because the most common Marid tier in material deep lakes. It lets survey reports sort trade-tier from noble Marid by dim luminescence without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Marid is the genus-rank grouping of water-element genie nobles of the Marid lineage within the True Elementals. Its members share a living-ocean water body with bioluminescent internal currents and deep-ocean colouration, and divide below into species groups by planar standing and ocean-territory type. The Marid genus covers all Marid lineage, defined by bioluminescent internal currents and ocean-blue colouration.
c.tax: Placed at genus rank, Marid is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a planar-origin living-ocean humanoid with bioluminescent internal currents. A new species belongs here only if it shows an ocean-water humanoid with bioluminescent currents and wish-granting architecture.
c.ap: The grouping is recognised by a vast blue-green water humanoid with internal luminescence. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — bioluminescent blue-green internal currents and deep-ocean smell — which survives even when the body disperses.
c.eco: Marid occupies the Elemental Plane of Water and deep underground seas. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where generated in the Elemental Plane of Water and recur with returns to Elemental Plane when killed, sustaining themselves on planar water-will.
c.beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: distant and powerful; tidal-event warning before force. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
c.traitsText: Shared characters: ocean-water body, bioluminescent currents, and wish-granting. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
c.abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: tidal events, water-travel, wish-granting, and passage-flooding. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
c.bg: In the Throat, Marid matters because bioluminescent pools in the deep tiers are the primary Marid field indicator. It lets survey reports sort Marid from Fluvial and Glacial elementals by bioluminescent currents without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.

c.g: Marid (Geniis aquae) is a water-element planar noble: a vast, sea-deep intelligence native to the Elemental Plane of Water, appearing as a towering humanoid of living ocean — a moving sea-column with a face. Documented from the Elemental Plane of Water and the deepest lake and ocean systems of the material world, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a humanoid body of living ocean water — dark blue-green, self-cohering, with internal bioluminescent currents — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The Marid is not a large Surger or Spume — its ocean-depth internal currents, bioluminescent glow, and planar origin place it in a distinct genie lineage. It does not form from sump pressure; it arrives from the deep. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a controlled disbanding into the surrounding water, leaving a blue-green bioluminescent glow that fades over hours and no carcass.
c.tax: Marid is placed at species rank beneath Marid (Marid Lords order, Marid class). Diagnosis rests on a living-ocean humanoid body with internal bioluminescent currents and ocean-depth colouration distinguishing it from all material-world water elementals. It is separated from its sibling Sump Surger by its ocean-water bioluminescent self-cohering body with planar origin versus the Surger's sump-water dark still-column bound to a specific pool; the two share the genus body-plan but never the sump-bound still-water formation. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: living ocean water animated by planar water-will, self-cohering at height without any pool to feed from.
c.ap: Marid stands 6–10 m, the largest of the genie classes typically. Its silhouette is a vast towering humanoid of blue-green ocean water with internal bioluminescent currents visible through the body. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: internal bioluminescent currents visible through the body — moving blue-green light inside a water column — which no material-world water elemental possesses; ocean-blue-green colouration rather than the dark sump-water or white aerated foam of material-world water elementals; and a smell of deep ocean — brine, depth-mineral, and ozone — rather than the mineral or volcanic smell of sump water. It moves with a slow towering stride that produces waves at 10 m, but without the floor-pressure of a Terrae form, and shows bioluminescent blue-green internal glow, 80 m range in darkness. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a trail of unusual mineral-blue water and bioluminescent marks on surrounding surfaces, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
c.eco: Marid persists in the Elemental Plane of Water, deep ocean systems, and the material world's deepest lake formations; they visit material lakes and underground seas, not surface sumps. It does not breed; it forms where born in the Elemental Plane of Water; not formed from sump pressure, and recurs whenever returns to the Elemental Plane when killed; does not re-form in a material pool. It sustains its mass on planar water-will, drawing in ocean minerals and elemental water rather than sump-water. Range is bounded by material visits bounded by planar contract: outside that band the body withdraws to the Elemental Plane. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as not applicable, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
c.beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Marid is roused by planar contract, summoning, or intrusion into their claimed underwater territories and otherwise lies a still blue-green water-column in a deep pool or underground sea, with a faint bioluminescent glow. It claims claimed underwater territories in deep material lakes and oceans and answers intrusion with tidal event warning — a sudden rise of water and localised current — before physical engagement. Toward grey-folk it is distant and powerful; survey crews report that Stillwardens have a saying: 'a bioluminescent pool in the deep tiers is either a Marid's resting ground or a sign of something worse'. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
c.traitsText: Stable characters of Marid: a body of living ocean water animated by planar water-will, self-cohering at height without any pool to feed from; immune to water and cold; the ocean-water body also resists freezing far more effectively than any Glacial elemental; and a defining weakness — lightning and fire, which disrupt the planar water architecture from the inside; and a broken territorial agreement. Its senses run through the medium itself: water-current sensing at oceanic range; the Marid can detect any significant water-body or creature movement within its claimed sea territory. It tolerates extreme cold, deep pressure, and darkness but fails at lightning and fire sustained at the body's interior. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, bioluminescent internal currents, ocean-blue colouration, and deep-ocean smell, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
c.abilities: Abilities of Marid are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it can create a tidal event around itself — a localised flood or current-wave extending 20 m; it can grant up to three wishes with a preference for water and sea-travel outcomes; it can travel through water at oceanic speed, covering the Gorge's entire water system in seconds; and it can breathe water into enclosed spaces, flooding passages in minutes. Each is a direct expression of planar water-will in a vast self-cohering ocean-water humanoid, so it cannot, for instance, be confused with a Surger by the bioluminescent currents; be affected by material-water elemental suppression; or be dried out like a Glacial. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
c.bg: In the Throat, Marid matters because a bioluminescent blue-green glow in a deep underground lake or sea is the definitive Marid presence indicator — if found in the Gorge, it would indicate a planar claim on the deep water system. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a planar-contact entry; not an active Gorge hazard; a bioluminescent pool is flagged with a sea-chalk mark, and its presence reshapes routes through deep underground lakes and seas, flagged with a blue-green chalk mark meaning 'possible Marid territory'. Stillwarden oral tradition holds that the underground sea's depth cannot be measured with a standard cord — a line that goes slack without touching bottom has reached a Marid's territory, not the actual floor.

c.eco: Elementals exists where its element is concentrated and mobile. Formation replaces breeding: pressure, flame, current, crystal growth, or mineral memory gathers into a moving body.
c.beh: Elementals behaviour is governed by elemental gradient: it seeks fuel, flow, pressure, open air, stone mass, or balance. Intelligence, if present, rides on that elemental drive.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include non-flesh substance, immunity to many biological harms, movement through or over its native element, and traces left as ash, wetness, grit, sparks, or pressure marks.
c.abilities: Elementals abilities are direct expressions of its element: burning, drowning, buffeting, crushing, shaping stone, freezing, corroding, or passing through matter according to substance.
c.bg: In the Throat, Elementals is a moving environmental condition as much as a creature, important for vents, floods, collapses, and Nadir physics.
c.note: Corrected placement: Elementals descend from Inner-Primordial energies directly, not from Proto-Material biological descent.

c.g: Creatures that were never alive — animated by negative energy from their origin, not transformed from living creatures. Distinguished from Undead (which are transformations of living creatures) by the fact that no living ancestor exists in their lineage.
c.tax: Animated by negative energy as original animating principle; no positive-energy ancestor anywhere in lineage; immunity to conditions requiring biological metabolism; theoretically could be 'reversed' by positive energy into a novel creature — a classification not yet discovered. Distinguished from Undead by the absence of any living ancestor.
c.ap: Shapes of darkness or negative-energy expression; no conventional anatomy; cold radiating presence; visible as organized darkness or absence-of-light rather than biological form.
c.note: Corrected placement: Unlife is a pre-vital Inner-Primordial condition and is not derived from the Proto-Material species.

c.g: Shadow creatures are unlife native to the extant Shadowfell: not merely dark animals, but beings whose anatomy is made from dimness, memory-loss, and negative reflection.
c.tax: Phylum subordinate to Unlife. Membership requires native Shadowfell origin or stable shadow-body physiology rather than ordinary corpse animation.
c.ap: Bodies are flattened toward silhouette, with edges that drink light and details that fade when stared at directly.
c.eco: They gather in light-starved passages, haunt old routes, and reproduce by severing or infecting living shadows.
c.beh: Most stalk patiently, preferring fear, isolation, and extinguished light over open pursuit.
c.traitsText: Light aversion, partial incorporeality, cold presence, and shadow-bonded movement.
c.abilities: Strength drain, shadow-step, light suppression, fear projection, or silhouette duplication depending on genus.
c.bg: They mark places where the Throat touches the Shadowfell rather than where ordinary darkness merely accumulates.

c.eco: Unlife occupies the niche implied by its own origin, anatomy, and recorded habitat. For field use, note diet or energy source, reproduction or formation, shelter, lifecycle, and what evidence remains after it passes.
c.beh: Unlife behaviour should be read from its own body and origin: how it moves, feeds, responds to threat, treats kin or rivals, and repeats habits in the field.
c.traitsText: Unlife traits include the size, senses, resistances, movement, intelligence, vulnerabilities, and diagnostic field marks belonging to this entry alone.
c.abilities: Unlife abilities are the natural, supernatural, magical, psionic, or extraordinary capacities actually supported by its own description. Text indicates: cold.
c.bg: The Throat records Unlife for field relevance: distribution, danger, ecological role, fossil meaning, cultural contact, or comparison value. Subordinate taxa: Proto-Unlife.

c.g: Fey are Inner-Primordial life shaped by primal nature-magic rather than by Outer-Planar alignment essence. They may touch the Feywild, bargains, courts, true names, and emotional geographies, but their source is the same inner substrate that makes living stone, water, root, crystal, and seasonal force biologically potent.
c.tax: Kingdom of Inner-Primordial primal organisms and spirits. Distinguished from Celestials and Fiends by the absence of constitutive good or evil alignment, and from Material plants or beasts by their true-name vulnerability, glamour ecology, bargain-binding, and non-standard mortality.
c.ap: Crystal Fey: 30 cm–1.5 m, quartz formations growing from joints, razor-edged crystal-sheet wings. Fog Fey: grey-folk-sized or smaller, soft-edged, never quite in focus. Both retain a quality of wrongness.
c.eco: Fey lives by bargain, season, emotion, dream, or wild-place resonance as much as by food. Its habitat is a rule of story attached to a grove, fog, threshold, revel, or grief-site.
c.beh: Fey behaviour follows etiquette, whim, debt, play, spite, or ancient compact. The safest records name the taboo and payment terms rather than merely describing combat ability.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include unnatural beauty or distortion, glamour traces, seasonal signs, odd shadows, altered footprints, and reactions to iron, names, promises, or hospitality.
c.abilities: Fey abilities may include glamour, charm, invisibility, teleporting through natural thresholds, curse bargains, dream influence, or weathering of memory according to the entry.
c.bg: In the Throat, Fey matters wherever old bargains and strange borders intersect with route safety.
c.note: Corrected from Outer-Primordial/Outsider placement. Fey are treated here as Inner-Primordial primal life, not alignment-essence outsiders.

c.g: Primal is the kingdom of ordinary embodied lineages in the Throat: beasts, plants, fungi, humanoids, giants, dragons, monstrosities, and other world-facing life whose ancestry is neither purely elemental, outer-planar, undead, fey, nor Far-Realm in origin.
c.tax: Kingdom-rank branch beneath Inner Primordial Life. This branch was formerly labelled Primal; Primal is used here because it better describes native embodied life and its deep-origin descendants without implying mundane surface biology only.
c.ap: Material organisms have persistent bodies with tissues, mineralized or organic structure, reproductive continuity, and ecological dependence on food, water, air, light, heat, or equivalent local resources.
c.eco: The kingdom contains producers, decomposers, animals, sapients, giants, dragons, constructs, oozes, and monstrosities, all interacting as a biosphere rather than as planar emanations.
c.beh: Behavior ranges from plant tropism to sapient culture, but all members can be studied through material constraints rather than only metaphysical alignment or elemental pressure.
c.traitsText: Embodied matter, local reproduction or manufacture, ecological dependency, and anatomy that can leave tracks, bones, spores, shells, shed skin, tools, or ruins.
c.abilities: Abilities are phylum-specific: photosynthesis, digestion, venom, tool use, giantism, draconic breath, crafted animation, or monstrous specialization.
c.bg: Renamed from Primal to Primal to better fit the catalogue’s taxonomy.

c.g: Proto-Material is the inferred stem species of ordinary material-plane biology: the first stable self-replicating body-line from which plants, fungi, beasts, humanoids, giants, dragons, monstrosities, constructs and related embodied organisms are catalogued. It does not include Elementals, Unlife, Fey, or Far-Realm life, which follow different primordial or extra-cosmological rules.
c.tax: Inferred species-level stem organism for material biological descent. Placement high in the tree reflects its ancestral importance, not a domain or kingdom rank. Elementals and Unlife are excluded because they arise from Inner-Primordial energetic and pre-vital conditions rather than ordinary material reproduction.
c.ap: No form recoverable. The taxon is a theoretical construct required by biochemical evidence: membranes, mineral-water chemistry, heredity, metabolism, and cellular or pseudo-cellular organization. It should not be reconstructed with tentacles, eyestalks, psionic organs, or other aberrant traits.
c.eco: Proto-Material represents the beginning of ordinary ecology: energy capture, replication, competition for chemical gradients, and inheritance through material bodies. Its descendants may later become magical, intelligent, undead-adjacent, or monstrous, but their life histories still make sense as transformations of material chemistry.
c.beh: No behavior is recoverable for Proto-Material itself. The field value of the taxon is comparative: if a creature eats, breeds, grows, leaves young, fossilizes, or mutates according to material constraints, it may belong somewhere below this line. If it breaks those constraints at the level of body logic, it belongs elsewhere.
c.traitsText: Proto-Material traits include the size, senses, resistances, movement, intelligence, vulnerabilities, and diagnostic field marks belonging to this entry alone. Recorded flags: fossil or inferred, theorized.
c.abilities: Proto-Material has no active powers in the creature sense. Its importance is the inherited capacity for metabolism, growth, reproduction, and adaptation. Later magic or supernatural traits in descendants are additions to material life, not evidence that the root itself possessed those powers.
c.bg: Within the Throat, this lineage anchors normal biology against which stranger forms are judged. It is the reason a mushroom, dragon, grey-folk child, cave eel, and mineral-rooted plant can still be compared as living things. Aberrations are now deliberately absent from its descendant list.
c.note: Rank corrected to Species. The journal uses tree position to show ancestry or derivation, while the rank label reflects taxonomic meaning.

c.g: Primary producers with cellulose cell walls and autotrophic metabolism. The Stonegrowth of the Throat represents the most extreme deep-adaptation of plant chemistry known to exist — mineral-lattice integration has replaced fluid vascular systems entirely.
c.tax: Plants phylum revised so former proto/sub-phyla containers no longer interrupt the hierarchy. Proto-Plants is retained as an inferred orphan species; observed lineages now sit directly beneath the phylum as classes.
c.ap: Surface: green to brown, leaf-bearing, light-seeking. Deep: mineral-integrated crystal-frond or thread forms; color silver to dark where mineral pigment dominates. All lack the cap-and-gill morphology of Fungi.
c.note: Revised branch: direct classes now sit under Plants; redundant proto order/sub-phylum containers were removed or converted.

c.g: The theorized common ancestor of all plant-kind, prior to the bryophyte/vascular divergence. Inferred from shared cellulose cell walls, autotrophic metabolism, and spore-based reproduction.
c.tax: Species-level inferred stem ancestor for the Plant branch. It is retained as an orphan reconstruction rather than as a formal container, so observed classes can sit directly beneath the Plant phylum.
c.ap: Proto-Plant is a plant-form organism; identify it by stem or frond structure, root anchorage, sap colour, leaf or tendril arrangement, reproductive bodies, and how it reacts to light, vibration, and touch.
c.eco: Proto-Plant lives by photosynthesis, chemosynthesis, parasitism, carrion trapping, or mineral uptake according to its described habitat. Reproduction occurs through seed, spore, runner, cutting, or magical propagation, with growth concentrated around water, light, heat, or nutrient seams.
c.beh: Proto-Plant behaviour is tropism and response: it turns, opens, closes, strikes, releases spores, or withholds fruit according to vibration, scent, heat, or light. Intelligence, if present, should be recorded as a separate trait rather than assumed from movement.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include anchorage, sap, tissue toughness, chemical defence, sensory hairs or fronds, and resistance to pain or blood loss. Damage patterns show tearing fibre, cut stems, resin, or pollen rather than animal bleeding.
c.abilities: Proto-Plant abilities may include grasping tendrils, toxins, thorns, camouflage, regeneration from roots, spore clouds, light production, or mineral hardening as appropriate to the described form.
c.bg: In the Throat, Proto-Plant matters by shaping food webs, choke points, medicine, poison, rope-fibre, shelter, or route hazards.

c.g: Non-vascular surface plants — mosses, liverworts, and hornworts. No confirmed endemic specimens in the Throat; the deep environment lacks the surface-light spectrum bryophytes require and they have not adapted as successfully as vascular plants to witchlight spectrum. Surface specimens occasionally carried in on equipment and materials persist briefly near the daylight shaft before dying.
c.tax: Non-vascular; cellulose cell walls; autotrophic; spore reproduction; requires moisture.
c.ap: Flat green mats, rarely more than 2 cm tall; surface-adapted entirely; no root system; spore reproduction; require liquid water contact rather than vascular transport.

c.g: Bryophyte-lineage plants that have achieved a partial adaptation to artificial-light spectrum — not true dark-adaptation (all confirmed specimens die within meters of the daylight shaft) but a shift in photosynthetic response sufficient to survive witchlight exposure longer than typical surface mosses. They occupy damp stone near the shaft rim and in the highest-lamp-density passages of the Works.
c.tax: Bryophyte with photosynthetic response shifted toward witchlight spectrum; requires water and light; non-vascular; spore reproduction. Distinguished from Surface-Flora vascular plants by non-vascular structure and flat-mat rather than upright growth form.
c.ap: Flat dark green mat 1–3 mm thick covering damp stone surfaces; slightly darker than surface mosses from the reduced light environment; texture slightly coarser from mineral deposition on the mat surface.

c.g: Moss Family is a rank-correction wrapper preserving existing entries while aligning the Shadow Mosses branch with the catalogue hierarchy.
c.tax: Family-rank placement created during the hierarchy cleanup so subordinate taxa occupy the expected tree position.
c.ap: See subordinate entries for diagnostic appearance.
c.eco: See subordinate entries for ecology.
c.beh: See subordinate entries for behavior.
c.traitsText: See subordinate entries for stable traits.
c.abilities: See subordinate entries for abilities.
c.bg: This entry was added to preserve hierarchy after removing redundant proto/container ranks.

c.g: Moss is a rank-correction wrapper preserving existing entries while aligning the Shadow Mosses branch with the catalogue hierarchy.
c.tax: Genus-rank placement created during the hierarchy cleanup so subordinate taxa occupy the expected tree position.
c.ap: See subordinate entries for diagnostic appearance.
c.eco: See subordinate entries for ecology.
c.beh: See subordinate entries for behavior.

c.g: The only confirmed Bryophyte with established population in the Gorge — a mat-forming moss that has shifted its photosynthetic response toward the blue-spectrum witchlight sufficiently to maintain a stable population on the damp walls of the daylight shaft below the witchlight-lamp line. It is the biological lower boundary of the surface-light zone. Below it, only the dark.
c.tax: Shadow Moss of daylight-shaft habitat; blue-spectrum photosynthetic adaptation; damp-stone surface requirement; the deepest confirmed Bryophyte in the Gorge and likely one of the deepest in any confirmed underdark system.
c.ap: Flat dark green mat covering damp shaft wall stone; approximately 2 mm thick; slightly luminescent in UV-adjacent witchlight from chlorophyll fluorescence; in ordinary lamplight appears as an unremarkable dark-green surface growth.
c.eco: Shaft Moss lives by photosynthesis, chemosynthesis, parasitism, carrion trapping, or mineral uptake according to its described habitat. Reproduction occurs through seed, spore, runner, cutting, or magical propagation, with growth concentrated around water, light, heat, or nutrient seams.
c.beh: Shaft Moss behaviour is tropism and response: it turns, opens, closes, strikes, releases spores, or withholds fruit according to vibration, scent, heat, or light. Intelligence, if present, should be recorded as a separate trait rather than assumed from movement.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include anchorage, sap, tissue toughness, chemical defence, sensory hairs or fronds, and resistance to pain or blood loss. Damage patterns show tearing fibre, cut stems, resin, or pollen rather than animal bleeding.
c.abilities: Shaft Moss abilities may include grasping tendrils, toxins, thorns, camouflage, regeneration from roots, spore clouds, light production, or mineral hardening as appropriate to the described form.
c.bg: In the Throat, Shaft Moss matters by shaping food webs, choke points, medicine, poison, rope-fibre, shelter, or route hazards.

c.beh: Shadow Mosses behaviour is tropism and response: it turns, opens, closes, strikes, releases spores, or withholds fruit according to vibration, scent, heat, or light. Intelligence, if present, should be recorded as a separate trait rather than assumed from movement.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include anchorage, sap, tissue toughness, chemical defence, sensory hairs or fronds, and resistance to pain or blood loss. Damage patterns show tearing fibre, cut stems, resin, or pollen rather than animal bleeding.
c.abilities: Shadow Mosses abilities may include grasping tendrils, toxins, thorns, camouflage, regeneration from roots, spore clouds, light production, or mineral hardening as appropriate to the described form.

c.eco: Bryophytes lives by photosynthesis, chemosynthesis, parasitism, carrion trapping, or mineral uptake according to its described habitat. Reproduction occurs through seed, spore, runner, cutting, or magical propagation, with growth concentrated around water, light, heat, or nutrient seams.
c.beh: Bryophytes behaviour is tropism and response: it turns, opens, closes, strikes, releases spores, or withholds fruit according to vibration, scent, heat, or light. Intelligence, if present, should be recorded as a separate trait rather than assumed from movement.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include anchorage, sap, tissue toughness, chemical defence, sensory hairs or fronds, and resistance to pain or blood loss. Damage patterns show tearing fibre, cut stems, resin, or pollen rather than animal bleeding.
c.abilities: Bryophytes abilities may include grasping tendrils, toxins, thorns, camouflage, regeneration from roots, spore clouds, light production, or mineral hardening as appropriate to the described form.
c.bg: In the Throat, Bryophytes matters by shaping food webs, choke points, medicine, poison, rope-fibre, shelter, or route hazards.

c.g: Plants with developed vascular systems for water and nutrient transport — the dominant surface plants. The Throat's Stonegrowth represents a radically divergent adaptation of vascular-plant chemistry to mineral rather than fluid transport.
c.tax: Vascular bundles (xylem and phloem or analogs) for internal transport; cellulose cell walls; autotrophic by photosynthesis or chemosynthesis.
c.ap: Massive range on the surface; in the deep, adapted forms tend toward mineral integration and reduced leaf-area.

c.g: Mineral-integrated Plants that grow in crystal formations — they are the biological layer of the Living Stone. The Stonesong Heralds harvest them carefully as biological relay-extensions for the signal network.
c.tax: Mineral lattice incorporation as primary structural element — the mineral lattice is load-bearing at the cellular level, not a coating. Cellulose-analog cell walls retain Plant classification.
c.ap: Crystal fronds 10–80 cm tall; mithril-vines appear as silver threads 1–3 mm diameter along passage walls. Easily mistaken for purely mineral formations.

c.g: Piezoelectric crystal-incorporating Plants that grow fern-like quartz-composite fronds and resonate with the Stonesong.
c.tax: Stonegrowth with quartz-composite tissue and active Stonesong resonance; fronds are simultaneously mineral and biological.
c.ap: 10–40 cm tall fronds; clear to smoky; ring audibly at specific Stonesong frequencies; arranged in rosettes or fans.

c.g: Frond is a rank-correction wrapper preserving existing entries while aligning the Crystal Fronds branch with the catalogue hierarchy.

c.g: The Singing Frond. Resonates at a depth-marker frequency used by the Stonesong Heralds. Grey-folk delvers say that when you first hear the Singing Frond, you are genuinely in the wild.
c.tax: Crystal Frond with active Stonesong resonance at Herald depth-marker frequency.
c.ap: 20–30 cm fan of clear quartz-composite fronds; sings faintly in Stonesong-active passages; individual fronds as thin and as sharp as glass.
c.eco: Singing Frond lives by photosynthesis, chemosynthesis, parasitism, carrion trapping, or mineral uptake according to its described habitat. Reproduction occurs through seed, spore, runner, cutting, or magical propagation, with growth concentrated around water, light, heat, or nutrient seams.
c.beh: Singing Frond behaviour is tropism and response: it turns, opens, closes, strikes, releases spores, or withholds fruit according to vibration, scent, heat, or light. Intelligence, if present, should be recorded as a separate trait rather than assumed from movement.

c.g: The Dark Frond. Incorporated iron rather than quartz; silent in the Stonesong but highly electroreceptive, responding to bioelectric fields by slowly turning toward their source. The Beast-Bonded use frond-turning to locate Wall-Horror territories before entering them.
c.tax: Crystal Frond with iron rather than quartz incorporation; bioelectric sensitivity rather than Stonesong resonance.
c.ap: 15–25 cm dark smoky-iron fronds; flexes slowly toward bioelectric sources; appears dark against lighter stone.
c.eco: Dark Frond lives by photosynthesis, chemosynthesis, parasitism, carrion trapping, or mineral uptake according to its described habitat. Reproduction occurs through seed, spore, runner, cutting, or magical propagation, with growth concentrated around water, light, heat, or nutrient seams.
c.beh: Dark Frond behaviour is tropism and response: it turns, opens, closes, strikes, releases spores, or withholds fruit according to vibration, scent, heat, or light. Intelligence, if present, should be recorded as a separate trait rather than assumed from movement.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include anchorage, sap, tissue toughness, chemical defence, sensory hairs or fronds, and resistance to pain or blood loss. Damage patterns show tearing fibre, cut stems, resin, or pollen rather than animal bleeding.
c.abilities: Dark Frond abilities may include grasping tendrils, toxins, thorns, camouflage, regeneration from roots, spore clouds, light production, or mineral hardening as appropriate to the described form.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dark Frond matters by shaping food webs, choke points, medicine, poison, rope-fibre, shelter, or route hazards.

c.g: The Dense Frond — a Crystal Frond growing in a particularly mineral-saturated zone where quartz deposition has produced an unusually thick crystal lattice. Its fronds are broad rather than fine, producing a lower-pitched Stonesong resonance that the Heralds use as a depth and mineral-density indicator.
c.tax: Crystal Frond of high-mineral-saturation growth zone; distinguished by thick frond cross-section (3–5 mm vs 0.5 mm for Cristallus velatus) and lower-frequency Stonesong resonance.
c.ap: 20–30 cm; fronds visibly broader and heavier than Cristallus velatus; produces a lower, slower ring when the Stonesong passes through; clusters feel dense when pushed past.
c.eco: Dense Frond lives by photosynthesis, chemosynthesis, parasitism, carrion trapping, or mineral uptake according to its described habitat. Reproduction occurs through seed, spore, runner, cutting, or magical propagation, with growth concentrated around water, light, heat, or nutrient seams.

c.g: Thread-like Plants growing along mithril grain-boundaries in passage walls, conducting the Stonesong across distances no relay-tower reaches.
c.tax: Stonegrowth specialized for mithril-grain-boundary colonization; conducts Stonesong as a biological relay; harvested threads remain conductive for weeks.
c.ap: Silver threads 1–3 mm diameter along passage walls; form networks following mithril veins; visible only by reflected light.

c.g: The Signal-Vine. Primary mithril-conducting Plant of Tier II; grows fast enough to bridge fresh-cut mithril surfaces within a shift. The Heralds use excised threads as biological relay-extensions.
c.tax: Mithril Vine with rapid post-cut regrowth; active Stonesong conduction.
c.ap: Signal-Vine is a plant-form organism; identify it by stem or frond structure, root anchorage, sap colour, leaf or tendril arrangement, reproductive bodies, and how it reacts to light, vibration, and touch.

c.g: The Hair-Vine. Thinner; infiltrates existing relay-mithril and sometimes carries a second, fainter signal the Heralds cannot account for. They have investigated this for three generations and prefer not to discuss their conclusions.
c.tax: Mithril Vine with secondary signal capacity; infiltrates rather than bridges.
c.ap: 0.5 mm diameter; nearly invisible individually; produces faint silver phosphorescence in active-mithril passages.

c.g: The Pale Signal-Vine — a Mithril Vine whose mithril substrate is a lower-purity alloy, reducing its conductivity and shifting the Stonesong signal it carries toward the detectable-but-degraded range the Heralds call noise. They harvest it deliberately as a source of intentional signal-degradation in passages they prefer to render unreadable to listeners with relay-adjacent capacity.
c.tax: Mithril Vine on low-purity mithril substrate; degraded conductivity; used deliberately as a signal-obscuring tool by the Stonesong Heralds.
c.ap: As Mithralia conductrix but with a slightly warmer, less purely silver colour from the alloy impurities; the phosphorescence it produces is amber-tinged rather than purely silver.
c.eco: Pale Signal-Vine lives by photosynthesis, chemosynthesis, parasitism, carrion trapping, or mineral uptake according to its described habitat. c.g: A mineral-integrated Plant whose crystal lattice is composed of a non-conductive mineral analog that suppresses Stonesong signal propagation in its immediate vicinity. The Heralds attempt to eradicate it wherever it appears in the relay network; it grows back. The Beast-Bonded use it to create signal-dark refuges for sensitive fauna.
c.tax: Stonegrowth Plant with non-conductive crystal lattice; Stonesong-suppressive rather than Stonesong-conductive.
c.ap: Grey-black flat crystals 5–15 cm tall in dense clusters; no resonance when touched; visually similar to Cristallus ater but entirely silent to Stonesong-sensitive equipment.
c.note: The Heralds consider Null-Weed a significant infrastructure hazard. The Beast-Bonded consider it an ecological tool. The disagreement is ongoing and occasionally heated.
c.eco: Null-Weed lives by photosynthesis, chemosynthesis, parasitism, carrion trapping, or mineral uptake according to its described habitat. Reproduction occurs through seed, spore, runner, cutting, or magical propagation, with growth concentrated around water, light, heat, or nutrient seams.

c.g: Introduced photosynthetic organisms descended from surface plants carried down by the grey-folk. Survive only within the Works witchlight footprint. Retreating precisely at the edge of manufactured light.
c.tax: Photosynthetic metabolism calibrated to surface-world sunlight spectrum; introduced rather than endemic; restricted to artificial-light zones. Distinguished from all other Throat Plants by green (not blue-green) coloration and surface-world leaf shapes.
c.ap: Dark green vines or rosettes; surface-world leaf shapes alien in the deep context; no flowers (no pollinators); reproduction by fragmentation.

c.g: The Shaft-Vine. A draped curtain-like vine growing from the rim of the daylight shaft. The lamplighters tend it, considering it the only living thing that still connects the city to the surface. They are correct.
c.tax: Surface-Flora adapted to the daylight shaft margin; reproduces by fragmentation; has grown continuously for the full length of the Heralds records.
c.ap: Dark green curtain-vine; leaves 2–4 cm, thick and waxy; hangs in sheets from the shaft rim.

c.g: The Lamp-Lichen. A flat photosynthetic organism adapted to witchlight spectrum so completely it survives in passages with only lamp-glow. Colonizes lamp-standard bases and over decades grows into the lamp mechanism itself.
c.tax: Surface-Flora adapted to witchlight spectrum; no daylight required; colonizes artificial light infrastructure.
c.ap: Dark green to olive flat crust; 5–15 cm patches on lamp-standard bases; slightly waxy.

c.g: A chemosynthetic plant-analog growing at Roil-vent openings, fixing sulfur compounds into organic matter the way surface plants fix carbon. Its 'petals' are sulfur-compound receptor surfaces resembling white-to-yellow flower shapes — the form maximizes sulfur-compound capture area, not pollinator attraction.
c.tax: Vascular Plant with chemosynthetic sulfur-compound fixation; Roil-vent habitat specialist.
c.ap: White-to-yellow rosette 10–25 cm diameter; petals translucent; grows in clusters of 3–8 at vent openings; the sulfur chemistry gives it a faint acrid smell that warns delvers of vent proximity.
c.eco: Vent Blossom lives by photosynthesis, chemosynthesis, parasitism, carrion trapping, or mineral uptake according to its described habitat. Reproduction occurs through seed, spore, runner, cutting, or magical propagation, with growth concentrated around water, light, heat, or nutrient seams.

c.g: Decomposers with chitin cell walls and heterotrophic metabolism. Formerly classified as Plants; the chitin vs. cellulose cell-wall distinction confirmed they represent a separate kingdom whose common ancestor with Plants predates both by a geological era.
c.tax: Fungi phylum revised so former proto/sub-phyla containers no longer interrupt the hierarchy. Proto-Fungi is retained as an inferred orphan species; observed lineages now sit directly beneath the phylum as classes.
c.ap: Surface: cap-and-gill morphology in Basidiomycota; cup-and-thread in Ascomycota. Deep Throat Fungi: bioluminescent ceiling mats, metal-chelating ore-prospectors, stone-eating lichen-analogs, acid-stable crust forms.
c.note: Revised branch: Fungi now contains three direct classes—Mycoforms, Molds, and True Fungi—plus Proto-Fungi as an inferred orphan species.

c.g: The theorized common ancestor of all fungi, prior to the Ascomycota/Basidiomycota/Zygomycota divergence. Inferred from shared chitin cell walls, heterotrophic metabolism, and spore reproduction.
c.tax: Species-level inferred stem ancestor for the Fungus branch. It is retained as an orphan reconstruction rather than as a formal container, so observed classes can sit directly beneath the Fungus phylum.
c.ap: Proto-Fungus is fungal; identify cap, shelf, mycelial mat, fruiting bodies, spore colour, odour, bruising reaction, and whether the visible organism is only a small part of a larger hidden network.
c.eco: Proto-Fungus feeds through mycelium, rot, mineral chemistry, symbiosis, or parasitism. Fruiting bodies are seasonal or triggered by moisture, corpses, heat, or disturbance; the visible cap may be less important than the hidden mat.
c.beh: Proto-Fungus behaviour is chemical and colonial: it blooms, releases spores, digests, signals through mycelium, or colonizes wounds and walls. Mobile or intelligent examples still retain fungal growth logic.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include spore print, smell, bruising colour, gill or pore structure, mycelial cords, toxin profile, and reaction to flame or salt. These traits remain useful after the cap is damaged.
c.abilities: Proto-Fungus abilities may include spores, hallucination, poison, rapid rot, bioluminescence, corpse digestion, symbiotic feeding, or mycelial communication depending on the entry.
c.bg: In the Throat, Proto-Fungus is food, medicine, toxin, light source, disease vector, or ecological recycler, and records should state which role it plays on a route.

c.g: Sentient fungal beings — the closest thing to a Fungus-kingdom civilisation. Myconids do not speak verbally; they communicate by releasing spore-packets that carry emotional and conceptual content directly to the olfactory cognition of any biological organism that breathes them. Their social structure is non-hierarchical by biology (all Myconids are physiologically equal) and becomes hierarchical only when the Circle elects a Sovereign for crisis-management.
c.tax: Sentient Basidiomycota of upright mobile body plan; spore-communication primary; no verbal language; Circle social structure; Sovereign elected for crisis; pacifist by default — Myconids do not attack unless the Circle decides the colony survival requires it.
c.ap: 60 cm–1.5 m upright; cap-and-stalk humanoid body plan; various cap colours by type; no eyes — sensory input from distributed surface spore-receptors; slow-moving; constantly emitting faint spore-cloud detectable as a sweet-earthy smell.

c.g: The adult Myconid — the primary social unit of the Circle. Releases rapport spores for communication and hallucination spores as a defence mechanism. The grey-folk's deepest Stonesong Heralds have established a tentative communication with a Myconid Circle in the Reaching, exchanging ecological information through spore-packet interpretation. The process is slow and the headaches are significant.
c.tax: Myconid adult of standard Circle-participation grade; rapport spore communication; hallucination spore defence; distinguished from Sprout by size and spore-communication capacity.
c.ap: 1 m; dark brown cap with white spots; stalk-body with arm-analogs; slow movement; sweet-earthy spore-cloud constant at 2 m radius.
c.eco: Myconid Adult feeds through mycelium, rot, mineral chemistry, symbiosis, or parasitism. Fruiting bodies are seasonal or triggered by moisture, corpses, heat, or disturbance; the visible cap may be less important than the hidden mat.
c.beh: Myconid Adult behaviour is chemical and colonial: it blooms, releases spores, digests, signals through mycelium, or colonizes wounds and walls. Mobile or intelligent examples still retain fungal growth logic.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include spore print, smell, bruising colour, gill or pore structure, mycelial cords, toxin profile, and reaction to flame or salt. These traits remain useful after the cap is damaged.
c.abilities: Myconid Adult abilities may include spores, hallucination, poison, rapid rot, bioluminescence, corpse digestion, symbiotic feeding, or mycelial communication depending on the entry.
c.bg: In the Throat, Myconid Adult is food, medicine, toxin, light source, disease vector, or ecological recycler, and records should state which role it plays on a route.

c.g: The Myconid Sovereign — the crisis-elected leader of the Circle. Larger than adults, capable of melding all Circle members into a shared consciousness for collective decision-making. The Sovereign the Heralds have encountered has been Sovereign continuously for forty years, which means the Circle has been in a continuous crisis state for forty years, which the Heralds find interesting and the Myconids find exhausting.
c.tax: Myconid of Sovereign-grade: largest body plan, meld-spore capacity allowing collective consciousness of entire Circle, crisis-election status.
c.ap: 1.5 m; large dark brown cap; proportionally larger cap-to-body ratio than adults; moves slowly and with apparent deliberation; the meld-spore release visible as a heavier silver-grey cloud at moments of decision.
c.eco: Myconid Sovereign feeds through mycelium, rot, mineral chemistry, symbiosis, or parasitism. Fruiting bodies are seasonal or triggered by moisture, corpses, heat, or disturbance; the visible cap may be less important than the hidden mat.
c.beh: Myconid Sovereign behaviour is chemical and colonial: it blooms, releases spores, digests, signals through mycelium, or colonizes wounds and walls. Mobile or intelligent examples still retain fungal growth logic.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include spore print, smell, bruising colour, gill or pore structure, mycelial cords, toxin profile, and reaction to flame or salt. These traits remain useful after the cap is damaged.
c.abilities: Myconid Sovereign abilities may include spores, hallucination, poison, rapid rot, bioluminescence, corpse digestion, symbiotic feeding, or mycelial communication depending on the entry.

c.tax: Class-rank fungal grouping for animate, social, or creature-like fungal forms such as myconids.

c.g: Molds are spreading, matting, or hazard-form fungal lineages. This class is retained for moldlike fungal bodies, but Violet Fungi has been moved to Deep Fungi.
c.tax: Class-rank fungal grouping for moldlike fungal hazards and surface-spreading fungal bodies.
