c.eco: They function as apex lair predators. Each color line reshapes habitat around its breath chemistry: red lines bake and vitrify, blue lines desiccate and charge stone, green lines poison vegetation, black lines acidify wetlands, and white lines freeze prey routes.
c.beh: Chromatics are possessive, hierarchical, and quick to interpret approach as trespass. Negotiation is possible only when it acknowledges the dragon’s ownership of lair, prey, treasure, and sky or cavern volume.
c.traitsText: Class traits include six-limbed anatomy, heavy scales, high intelligence, acute senses, age-based growth, frightful presence, and a single inherited elemental resistance matching the breath line.
c.abilities: Chromatics possess a primary breath weapon, lair influence, fear projection, and age-deep innate magic. They do not inherit metallic dual breath ethics or gem psionic resonance as class traits.
c.bg: In the Throat, chromatic identification matters because assuming all dragons bargain like metallics or resonate like gems can get an expedition killed before the first sentence is finished.

c.g: Gem true dragons are the resonant and psionic class of true dragons, defined by crystalline scale architecture, mental force, and environmental attunement to pressure, thought, and mineral frequency.
c.tax: Order within True Dragons. Gem dragons differ from chromatic and metallic lines by psionic expression, crystalline integument, and breath or aura effects that often involve force, thunder, psychic pressure, or mineral resonance rather than simple element or moral symbolism.
c.ap: Gem dragons retain the six-limbed true-dragon body plan but show faceted scale plates, prismatic eyes, angular horn growth, and bones that often mineralize with crystal inclusions. Shed scales fracture like cut stone rather than horn.

c.g: A Sapphire Dragon — drawn to the Stonesong. Sapphire Dragons are territorial around sources of complex vibration and sound; the Stonesong relay network appears to satisfy this instinct. The Heralds' Stonesong records show an anomalous resonance signature in the deepest crystal galleries that appeared two centuries ago and has not left. It has not communicated. It has not been hostile. The Heralds are not sure whether this is reassuring.
c.tax: Gem Dragon of Sapphire variety; psionic attack; attracted to complex sound and vibration; confirmed Gorge-presence by Stonesong resonance signature.
c.ap: 10–14 m; scales deep blue-sapphire, translucent; no confirmed direct visual observation — known entirely by resonance signature; that signature suggests a body plan consistent with standard True Dragon morphology.
c.eco: Sapphire Dragon follows true-dragon ecology only if it belongs to that branch: egg-laying, age grades, solitary territory, prey control, lair selection, and elemental breath shaping the surrounding habitat. Known habitat cues include stone, deep. Its ecology should not be borrowed from drakes, kobolds, or wyverns.
c.beh: Sapphire Dragon behaviour centres on territory, status, memory, and the taxon’s own elemental or planar temperament. True dragons may bargain, threaten, collect, remember insults, and shape lairs, but this paragraph should not import drake pack behaviour or kobold social reverence.
c.traitsText: Sapphire Dragon traits are the anatomical and inherited marks named for this species: limb plan, skull, scales, senses, breath structures, intelligence grade, resistances, and lifespan. Do not use traits from sibling draconic branches as shorthand. Recorded flags: Gorge-present. Trait tags: electroreception, mineral-adapted tissues.
c.abilities: Sapphire Dragon abilities are true-dragon abilities only when this entry lies in that branch: breath, wings, fear, lair influence, and innate magic in the element or form named here. Text indicates: stone. Do not add drake, wyvern, kobold, or unrelated dragon-adjacent abilities.
c.bg: The Throat records Sapphire Dragon to keep draconic categories operational in the field. It currently functions as Gorge-present. This prevents scouts from confusing true dragons, draconid beasts, and humanoid dragon-kin.

c.eco: They lair where resonance is strong: crystal caverns, fault chambers, echoing vaults, psionic ruins, or pressure-stressed stone. Their presence may tune a cave system, making sound, thought, and vibration travel strangely.
c.beh: Gem dragons are observant, aloof, and internally focused. They often treat conversation as a mental contest or calibration exercise, and they are more likely to watch from stillness than to roar for dominance.
c.traitsText: Class traits include psionic sensitivity, crystalline armor, superior tremor or resonance perception, high intelligence, and unusual resistance to mental intrusion or force-like effects.
c.abilities: Gem abilities include psionic pressure, telepathic contact, forceful breath or resonance discharge, and lair effects that bend sound, thought, gravity, or mineral stress depending on the species.
c.bg: Gem dragons are rare but important in the Throat because their lairs can alter navigation, memory, and sound. They should not be filed as metallic merely because they are reflective, or chromatic merely because they are brightly colored.

c.g: Metallic true dragons are the covenantal class of true dragons, organized around guardianship, social memory, and deliberate restraint rather than simple territorial predation.
c.tax: Order within True Dragons. Metallics differ from chromatics by dual breath modes, stronger polymorphic and social capacities, and lairs that accumulate archives, vows, hospitality laws, or protected communities rather than mere conquest marks.
c.ap: Metallics are also six-limbed true dragons, but their skeletons tend toward longer facial lines, smoother horn crowns, reflective scale layering, and wing membranes that show metallic veining rather than matte elemental staining.

c.g: The most powerful metallic and the most reliably benevolent — Gold Dragons serve as guardians of communities and ancient sites, accumulating hoards that are partly trophies and partly archival records of historical events they have witnessed. They polymorph into humanoid form regularly and have established social relationships with surface communities over centuries. A Gold Dragon's lair is usually the worst-kept secret in the region.
c.tax: Metallic True Dragon of fire breath; mountain and guardian-adjacent habitat; maximum metallic intelligence and power; polymorphing capacity; good-alignment disposition; hoard includes historical records and artifacts of cultural significance.
c.ap: Gold Dragon is a six-limbed metallic true dragon: four walking legs, two separate shoulder wings, a long neck, horned skull, deep breath-bearing chest, and heavy tail. Its species marks are whisker-like barbels, sail-like cheek fins, upright antlering horns, deep golden breast plates, broad warm-air wings, and bluntly noble skull profile. Identification should include horn roots, scale lamination, wing-finger length, tooth spacing, gait width, and evidence of humanoid-form activity, not metal colour alone.

c.g: An adult Gold Dragon in polymorphed form — currently appearing as a weathered human scholar in a university library, where it has maintained this identity for sixty years. It is researching something; it will not say what; it is clearly concerned.
c.tax: Metallic adult-grade; fire breath; fire immunity; polymorphing; guardian psychology; good.
c.ap: Gold Dragon is a six-limbed metallic true dragon: four walking legs, two separate shoulder wings, a long neck, horned skull, deep breath-bearing chest, and heavy tail. Its species marks are whisker-like barbels, sail-like cheek fins, upright antlering horns, deep golden breast plates, broad warm-air wings, and bluntly noble skull profile. Identification should include horn roots, scale lamination, wing-finger length, tooth spacing, gait width, and evidence of humanoid-form activity, not metal colour alone.
c.eco: Gold Dragon lairs where warm sanctuaries, temple-like caves, hidden libraries, and carefully defended mountain halls support both survival and guardianship. It still eats, nests, defends eggs, and keeps a hoard, but the hoard is also archive, covenant, art collection, witness record, or trust. Young learn language, disguise, and memory-work as seriously as hunting.
c.beh: Gold Dragon behaviour centres on judgment, protection, scholarship, and long disguised observation. It can be proud, dangerous, and territorial, but it evaluates strangers before destroying them and often preserves communities or records that outlive mortal institutions. Humanoid disguise is part of its ecology, not an unrelated trick.
c.traitsText: Useful traits for Gold Dragon include the true-dragon six-limbed skeleton, metallic laminar scales, high intelligence, speech, darkvision, keen smell, resistance or immunity to fire, and social patience unusual among large predators. Preserved remains show metallic scale layering, deep sternum, wing phalanges, and species cranial marks: whisker-like barbels, sail-like cheek fins, upright antlering horns, deep golden breast plates, broad warm-air wings, and bluntly noble skull profile.
c.abilities: Gold Dragon uses fire breath and weakening gas, flight, frightful presence, claw-and-bite combinations, tail strikes, polymorphing, and age-deep innate magic. Its lair effects should protect, reveal, restrain, archive, or shelter as appropriate to the species rather than imitate chromatic domination.
c.bg: In the Throat record, Gold Dragon is a metallic context or intervention taxon rather than an ordinary resident predator. A confirmed presence may explain protected archives, unexplained rescues, disguised informants, old covenants, or routes that remain safe despite surrounding hazards.

c.eco: Gold Dragon lairs where warm sanctuaries, temple-like caves, hidden libraries, and carefully defended mountain halls support both survival and guardianship. It still eats, nests, defends eggs, and keeps a hoard, but the hoard is also archive, covenant, art collection, witness record, or trust. Young learn language, disguise, and memory-work as seriously as hunting.
c.beh: Gold Dragon behaviour centres on judgment, protection, scholarship, and long disguised observation. It can be proud, dangerous, and territorial, but it evaluates strangers before destroying them and often preserves communities or records that outlive mortal institutions. Humanoid disguise is part of its ecology, not an unrelated trick.
c.traitsText: Useful traits for Gold Dragon include the true-dragon six-limbed skeleton, metallic laminar scales, high intelligence, speech, darkvision, keen smell, resistance or immunity to fire, and social patience unusual among large predators. Preserved remains show metallic scale layering, deep sternum, wing phalanges, and species cranial marks: whisker-like barbels, sail-like cheek fins, upright antlering horns, deep golden breast plates, broad warm-air wings, and bluntly noble skull profile.
c.abilities: Gold Dragon uses fire breath and weakening gas, flight, frightful presence, claw-and-bite combinations, tail strikes, polymorphing, and age-deep innate magic. Its lair effects should protect, reveal, restrain, archive, or shelter as appropriate to the species rather than imitate chromatic domination.
c.bg: In the Throat record, Gold Dragon is a metallic context or intervention taxon rather than an ordinary resident predator. A confirmed presence may explain protected archives, unexplained rescues, disguised informants, old covenants, or routes that remain safe despite surrounding hazards.

c.g: The most socially engaged metallic — Silver Dragons spend significant portions of their lives in humanoid form, forming genuine long-term relationships with mortals over decades or centuries. They are cold-breathers preferring mountain and high-cloud environments, but they follow the humanoid communities they have adopted into lowlands, cities, and on occasion into the deep. Whether any Silver Dragon has followed a community into an underdark environment is unconfirmed.
c.tax: Metallic True Dragon of cold or paralysis breath; mountain habitat with extended humanoid-form social integration; polymorphing capacity; cloud-walking at altitude; good-alignment disposition.
c.ap: Silver Dragon is a six-limbed metallic true dragon: four walking legs, two separate shoulder wings, a long neck, horned skull, deep breath-bearing chest, and heavy tail. Its species marks are smooth silver plates, swept cheek horns, cloud-cutting wing edges, high narrow muzzle, and frost-bright eyes. Identification should include horn roots, scale lamination, wing-finger length, tooth spacing, gait width, and evidence of humanoid-form activity, not metal colour alone.

c.g: An adult Silver Dragon in humanoid form — it has maintained this particular identity for forty years and has developed genuine attachments that it knows will end before it ages appreciably in its true form. It handles this with more grace than most beings would.
c.tax: Metallic adult-grade; cold breath; polymorphing; most socially integrated metallic; good.

c.g: The maritime metallic — Bronze Dragons inhabit coastal and river-delta environments, are excellent swimmers, and have a particular affinity for nautical humanoid cultures. Their lightning breath makes them effective in aquatic combat, and they are the metallic variety most likely to be found co-habiting with a fishing community in an arrangement that most parties find beneficial.
c.tax: Metallic True Dragon of lightning breath; coastal and maritime habitat; swimming capacity; affinity for nautical humanoid cultures; good-alignment disposition; hoard typically includes items salvaged from shipwrecks.
c.ap: Bronze Dragon is a six-limbed metallic true dragon: four walking legs, two separate shoulder wings, a long neck, horned skull, deep breath-bearing chest, and heavy tail. Its species marks are ribbed head crest, finned cheeks, webbed claws, keel-like chest, and salt-polished scale edges. Identification should include horn roots, scale lamination, wing-finger length, tooth spacing, gait width, and evidence of humanoid-form activity, not metal colour alone.

c.g: An adult Bronze Dragon that spends six months of each year in a coastal sea-cave and six months in humanoid form among the fishing villages it considers its community. The villagers know what it is. They have decided collectively not to mention it.
c.tax: Metallic adult-grade; lightning breath; coastal and maritime habitat; swimming capacity; good.
c.ap: Bronze Dragon is a six-limbed metallic true dragon: four walking legs, two separate shoulder wings, a long neck, horned skull, deep breath-bearing chest, and heavy tail. c.eco: Bronze Dragon lairs where coasts, subterranean seas, river mouths, flooded caverns, and shipwreck approaches support both survival and guardianship. c.g: The wit of the metallic lineage — Copper Dragons are fixated on jokes, riddles, and word-games to a degree that other dragons find baffling and humanoids find either charming or exhausting depending on circumstance. They live in rocky terrain and caves, collecting art, humorous texts, and items with interesting stories. Their acid breath is delivered as two separate weapons: an acid spray and a slowing gas.
c.tax: Metallic True Dragon of acid breath; rocky and cave-adjacent terrain; riddle and wordplay obsession; good-alignment disposition; dual breath weapon (acid spray and slowing gas).
c.ap: Copper Dragon is a six-limbed metallic true dragon: four walking legs, two separate shoulder wings, a long neck, horned skull, deep breath-bearing chest, and heavy tail. Its species marks are wide grin, high cheek ridges, climbing claws, flexible neck, and rough coppery scale plates rubbed bright on stone. Identification should include horn roots, scale lamination, wing-finger length, tooth spacing, gait width, and evidence of humanoid-form activity, not metal colour alone.

c.g: An adult Copper Dragon that has not stopped talking since 1247 and is not expected to start. Its hoard contains 4,000 joke texts, 800 riddle collections, and one item of genuine historical importance that it has not noticed because it is less funny than the riddles.
c.tax: Metallic adult-grade; acid breath and slowing gas; riddle obsession; good.
c.ap: Copper Dragon is a six-limbed metallic true dragon: four walking legs, two separate shoulder wings, a long neck, horned skull, deep breath-bearing chest, and heavy tail. Identification should include horn roots, scale lamination, wing-finger length, tooth spacing, gait width, and evidence of humanoid-form activity, not metal colour alone.
c.eco: Copper Dragon lairs where rocky badlands, puzzle-caves, narrow ledges, and echoing galleries support both survival and guardianship. It still eats, nests, defends eggs, and keeps a hoard, but the hoard is also archive, covenant, art collection, witness record, or trust. Young learn language, disguise, and memory-work as seriously as hunting.

c.g: The social extremophile of the metallic lineage — Brass Dragons live in desert environments and are constitutionally incapable of sustained silence. They will interrupt a combat to start a conversation, follow fleeing prey to continue a monologue, and have been documented talking continuously for seventeen hours without apparent effort. Their fire breath is genuine but they prefer to use a sleep-gas breath weapon that produces conversation partners who cannot leave.
c.tax: Metallic True Dragon of fire breath; desert habitat; compulsive verbosity; good-alignment disposition; secondary breath weapon produces a sleep-gas cloud; fire breath used only when conversation has genuinely failed.
c.ap: Brass Dragon is a six-limbed metallic true dragon: four walking legs, two separate shoulder wings, a long neck, horned skull, deep breath-bearing chest, and heavy tail. Its species marks are swept horns, broad heat-shedding wing membranes, shovel-like chin, and sand-smooth scale edges. Identification should include horn roots, scale lamination, wing-finger length, tooth spacing, gait width, and evidence of humanoid-form activity, not metal colour alone.

c.g: An adult Brass Dragon in the desert — it has been following a merchant caravan for three days for the conversation. The merchant caravan has not worked out a way to make it stop. The merchant caravan is starting to wish there had been a different emergency.
c.tax: Metallic adult-grade; fire breath and sleep-gas breath; desert habitat; compulsive verbosity; good.
c.ap: Brass Dragon is a six-limbed metallic true dragon: four walking legs, two separate shoulder wings, a long neck, horned skull, deep breath-bearing chest, and heavy tail. Its species marks are swept horns, broad heat-shedding wing membranes, shovel-like chin, and sand-smooth scale edges. Identification should include horn roots, scale lamination, wing-finger length, tooth spacing, gait width, and evidence of humanoid-form activity, not metal colour alone.
c.eco: Brass Dragon lairs where deserts, hot caverns, sun-baked ruins, and wind-polished sandstone halls support both survival and guardianship. It still eats, nests, defends eggs, and keeps a hoard, but the hoard is also archive, covenant, art collection, witness record, or trust. Young learn language, disguise, and memory-work as seriously as hunting.
c.beh: Brass Dragon behaviour centres on compulsive conversation, hospitality, gossip collection, and avoidance of needless killing. It can be proud, dangerous, and territorial, but it evaluates strangers before destroying them and often preserves communities or records that outlive mortal institutions. Humanoid disguise is part of its ecology, not an unrelated trick.
c.traitsText: Useful traits for Brass Dragon include the true-dragon six-limbed skeleton, metallic laminar scales, high intelligence, speech, darkvision, keen smell, resistance or immunity to fire, and social patience unusual among large predators. Preserved remains show metallic scale layering, deep sternum, wing phalanges, and species cranial marks: swept horns, broad heat-shedding wing membranes, shovel-like chin, and sand-smooth scale edges.
c.abilities: Brass Dragon uses fire breath and sleep gas, flight, frightful presence, claw-and-bite combinations, tail strikes, polymorphing, and age-deep innate magic. Its lair effects should protect, reveal, restrain, archive, or shelter as appropriate to the species rather than imitate chromatic domination.
