c.bg: In the Throat, Fog-Daemon matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.g: The War-Daemon. Feeds on open violence. Found at active clan conflict margins. The small horns barely visible through hair, the claws on the off-hand, and the too-many teeth are all missed the same way.
c.tax: Fog-Fiend in grey-folk disguise; identifiable by small horns, clawed off-hand, and excess dentition.
c.ap: 2.2 m; heavily built; small horns visible through hair; off-hand clawed; teeth slightly too many; carries two weapons it did not make.
c.eco: War-Daemon persists where its lower-planar appetite has purchase: souls, fear, contracts, blood, ruin, corruption, or violence. It does not simply fill an animal niche; it engineers conditions that feed its nature.
c.beh: War-Daemon behaviour follows its fiendish specialization: bargaining, predation, temptation, command, ambush, or mercenary service as described by the entry. It recognizes advantage and retreats from losses that do not feed its purpose.

c.g: A sub-family of Tanar'ri that feeds directly on life-force rather than on souls or suffering — they are among the most immediately dangerous fiends in a combat context because their feeding restores their own vitality. Encountered in the Fogreach attempting to establish a permanent feeding ground in the war-grief between clans.
c.tax: Tanar'ri sub-family distinguished by direct life-force feeding mechanic; regenerates damage through feeding; classified at Family rather than Phylum because they share Tanar'ri ancestry.
c.ap: 1.8–2.2 m; dark red-black; translucent patches over major blood vessels visible from outside; mouth disproportionately wide.

c.g: The Blood Fiend. Drains life through touch; heals from the drain. Three of them have established a territory in the deepest Fogreach killing-ground between the Wall-Wardens and the deepest Troglodyte range. The Pall-Brokers have been very busy in that corridor.
c.tax: Blood Fiend of confirmed Gorge territory; territorial; drains through sustained contact rather than single strike.
c.ap: 2 m; dark red-black; the translucent patches over major vessels glow faintly when it has recently fed.
c.eco: Blood Fiend persists where its lower-planar appetite has purchase: souls, fear, contracts, blood, ruin, corruption, or violence. It does not simply fill an animal niche; it engineers conditions that feed its nature.
c.beh: Blood Fiend behaviour follows its fiendish specialization: bargaining, predation, temptation, command, ambush, or mercenary service as described by the entry. It recognizes advantage and retreats from losses that do not feed its purpose.

c.bg: In the Throat, Blood Fiend matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.g: Fiends of Gehenna — neutral evil outsiders who serve only profit. Mercenaries of the lower planes; willing to work for either Baatezu or Tanar'ri at the right price.
c.tax: Neutral evil outsider; Gehenna-origin; no inherent allegiance; nature is mercenary by constitution.
c.ap: Variable; generally humanoid-to-monstrous range; less immediately threatening in appearance than Baatezu or Tanar'ri — they want to be hired, not feared on sight.

c.g: The infantry Yugoloth — insectoid fiends that serve as the Yugoloth's foot-soldiers in planar mercenary contracts. They are the least expensive Yugoloth to hire and correspondingly the least reliable, following contracts to the letter and exploiting every gap in specification. A Mezzoloth employed to guard a door will guard the door; it will not notice anything happening on the other side of the wall.
c.tax: Neutral evil Yugoloth of insectoid form; foot-soldier grade; contract-service economy; immune to acid and poison; teleportation capacity. Distinguished from Baatezu and Tanar'ri by mercenary rather than inherent-evil motivation.
c.ap: 1.8 m; insectoid bipedal form; four arms (two weapon-bearing, two manipulatory); chitinous dark grey-green exoskeleton; three-lens compound eyes; carries a trident as standard issue; smells of something between rotting and chemical.
c.eco: Mezzoloth persists where its lower-planar appetite has purchase: souls, fear, contracts, blood, ruin, corruption, or violence. It does not simply fill an animal niche; it engineers conditions that feed its nature.
c.beh: Mezzoloth behaviour follows its fiendish specialization: bargaining, predation, temptation, command, ambush, or mercenary service as described by the entry. It recognizes advantage and retreats from losses that do not feed its purpose.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include evil-aligned aura, unnatural heat or cold, horns or claws where present, resistance to mundane harm, planar scent, and the specific body marks named in appearance.
c.abilities: Mezzoloth abilities may include teleportation, poison, fire, fear, disguise, contract magic, life-drain, or summoning only where the entry or lineage indicates that specialization.
c.bg: In the Throat, Mezzoloth matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.g: Gargoyle-form Yugoloths — the aerial assault and elite-operative grade of the Yugoloth hierarchy. They serve as scouts, assassins, and field commanders and are considerably more intelligent than Mezzoloths, which means they exploit contract gaps at a correspondingly higher level. A Nycaloth commissioned to kill someone will kill that specific someone; it will not necessarily prevent them from being warned first.
c.tax: Neutral evil Yugoloth of gargoyle form; aerial capacity via wings; regeneration of moderate grade; teleportation capacity; elite-operative grade above Mezzoloth. Known for green skin and large wings.
c.ap: 2.4 m; green-skinned humanoid with large leather wings and two horns; muscular; carries an axe; the expression is of professional attention, which is more alarming than malice.
c.eco: Nycaloth persists where its lower-planar appetite has purchase: souls, fear, contracts, blood, ruin, corruption, or violence. It does not simply fill an animal niche; it engineers conditions that feed its nature.
c.beh: Nycaloth behaviour follows its fiendish specialization: bargaining, predation, temptation, command, ambush, or mercenary service as described by the entry. It recognizes advantage and retreats from losses that do not feed its purpose.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include evil-aligned aura, unnatural heat or cold, horns or claws where present, resistance to mundane harm, planar scent, and the specific body marks named in appearance.
c.abilities: Nycaloth abilities may include teleportation, poison, fire, fear, disguise, contract magic, life-drain, or summoning only where the entry or lineage indicates that specialization.
c.bg: In the Throat, Nycaloth matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.g: Jackal-headed wizard-grade Yugoloths — the diplomats, administrators, and magical specialists of the Yugoloth hierarchy. They write and keep contracts across the planes, have perfect memory for every agreement they have participated in, and charge significantly for their services precisely because their memory and their magical capacity make those services genuinely valuable. They are not hired for combat.
c.tax: Neutral evil Yugoloth of jackal-headed humanoid form; spellcaster grade; contract-administration specialty; immune to acid, fire, and poison; teleportation; perfect contract memory as defining characteristic.
c.ap: 1.8 m; jackal-headed humanoid; elegant robes over slim build; always carrying writing materials; the eyes track everything with the attention of someone calculating its value in an ongoing negotiation.
c.eco: Arcanoloth persists where its lower-planar appetite has purchase: souls, fear, contracts, blood, ruin, corruption, or violence. It does not simply fill an animal niche; it engineers conditions that feed its nature.
c.beh: Arcanoloth behaviour follows its fiendish specialization: bargaining, predation, temptation, command, ambush, or mercenary service as described by the entry. It recognizes advantage and retreats from losses that do not feed its purpose.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include evil-aligned aura, unnatural heat or cold, horns or claws where present, resistance to mundane harm, planar scent, and the specific body marks named in appearance.
c.abilities: Arcanoloth abilities may include teleportation, poison, fire, fear, disguise, contract magic, life-drain, or summoning only where the entry or lineage indicates that specialization.
c.bg: In the Throat, Arcanoloth matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.g: Malevolent spirit-outsiders of Persian cosmological tradition — not native to the Nine Hells or the Abyss but to their own plane of corruption, Dozakhan. They are specialists in specific categories of ruin: Doru Divs corrupt water, Aghash Divs corrupt fate, Shira Divs corrupt the natural world. They interact with other fiendish traditions only when the corruption categories overlap.
c.tax: Outer-planar evil outsiders of Dozakhan origin; distinguished from Baatezu and Tanar'ri by plane-of-origin and specialisation in specific corruption categories rather than general evil.
c.ap: Appear as large (2–3 m) humanoid shapes of corrupted natural material — rotting wood, fouled water, diseased flesh — appropriate to their corruption category.

c.g: A Doru Div — corruptor of water. Its presence in the Gorge represents a significant threat to the Three Waters. The Stillwardens have standing contingency protocols for Doru Div incursion.
c.tax: Div of water-corruption specialisation; distinguished by aquatic form and the fouling of any water source it contacts for weeks after departure.
c.ap: 2.5 m; vaguely humanoid shape of dark brackish water that does not disperse; smells of something wrong in a way that bypasses rational evaluation.
c.eco: Doru Div persists where its lower-planar appetite has purchase: souls, fear, contracts, blood, ruin, corruption, or violence. It does not simply fill an animal niche; it engineers conditions that feed its nature.
c.beh: Doru Div behaviour follows its fiendish specialization: bargaining, predation, temptation, command, ambush, or mercenary service as described by the entry. It recognizes advantage and retreats from losses that do not feed its purpose.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include evil-aligned aura, unnatural heat or cold, horns or claws where present, resistance to mundane harm, planar scent, and the specific body marks named in appearance.
c.abilities: Doru Div abilities may include teleportation, poison, fire, fear, disguise, contract magic, life-drain, or summoning only where the entry or lineage indicates that specialization.
c.bg: In the Throat, Doru Div matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.g: Evil outsiders of material-plane origin who achieve fiend-status through accumulated karmic corruption across multiple lifetimes. Unlike Baatezu or Tanar'ri they are not native to any lower plane — they are the material plane's own production of evil, cycling through death and reincarnation until the corruption is total. They appear in the Gorge only rarely and only with specific purpose, which is always worse than it appears.
c.tax: Outer-planar evil outsiders of material-plane karmic origin; distinguished from Baatezu and Tanar'ri by the reincarnation lifecycle; immune to non-epic magic; shapeshifting primary capacity; true form is an animal-headed humanoid with backwards hands.
c.ap: Appear as wealthy, elegant humanoids until the backwards hands are noticed. True form: animal-headed (tiger most common) with reversed palms; immaculate dress regardless of environment.

c.g: The standard Rakshasa — one iteration from its final death and fully aware of it. In the Gorge to arrange something whose nature will not be apparent until it is too late.
c.tax: Rakshasa of penultimate-incarnation status; at maximum power and minimum restraint.
c.ap: Appears as a grey-folk merchant of unusual wealth; backwards hands concealed in gloves; the gloves are the tell for anyone who knows to look.
c.eco: Operative Rakshasa persists where its lower-planar appetite has purchase: souls, fear, contracts, blood, ruin, corruption, or violence. It does not simply fill an animal niche; it engineers conditions that feed its nature.
c.beh: Operative Rakshasa behaviour follows its fiendish specialization: bargaining, predation, temptation, command, ambush, or mercenary service as described by the entry. It recognizes advantage and retreats from losses that do not feed its purpose.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include evil-aligned aura, unnatural heat or cold, horns or claws where present, resistance to mundane harm, planar scent, and the specific body marks named in appearance.
c.abilities: Operative Rakshasa abilities may include teleportation, poison, fire, fear, disguise, contract magic, life-drain, or summoning only where the entry or lineage indicates that specialization.
c.bg: In the Throat, Operative Rakshasa matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.bg: In the Throat, Rakshasas matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.g: The primordial demons — pre-dating the Tanar'ri, pre-dating the differentiation of the Abyss into layers. They are what the Abyss was before it organised itself. Their form is genuinely alien in a way that distinguishes them from even the most horrifying Tanar'ri: not monstrous-humanoid, not animal-hybridised, simply wrong in a geometric sense.
c.tax: Outer-planar evil outsiders of primordial Abyssal origin; pre-date Tanar'ri; distinguished by non-humanoid, non-animal-hybrid body plan with geometry that does not resolve correctly at any viewing angle; cause sanity damage by being perceived.
c.ap: Variable. No confirmed shared morphology. Common feature: something in their shape does not make sense in three dimensions and the eye cannot stop trying.

c.g: A primordial Obyrith theorised to have drifted from the deepest Abyss into the Nadir margin. The Cult of Nothing has encountered it; the Cult's records of the encounter use no words, only geometric figures.
c.tax: Obyrith of suspected Nadir-margin presence; theorised from secondary evidence only.
c.ap: Unknown. The Cult's geometric records suggest something octahedral that breathes.
c.eco: Deep Obyrith persists where its lower-planar appetite has purchase: souls, fear, contracts, blood, ruin, corruption, or violence. It does not simply fill an animal niche; it engineers conditions that feed its nature.
c.beh: Deep Obyrith behaviour follows its fiendish specialization: bargaining, predation, temptation, command, ambush, or mercenary service as described by the entry. It recognizes advantage and retreats from losses that do not feed its purpose.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include evil-aligned aura, unnatural heat or cold, horns or claws where present, resistance to mundane harm, planar scent, and the specific body marks named in appearance.
c.abilities: Deep Obyrith abilities may include teleportation, poison, fire, fear, disguise, contract magic, life-drain, or summoning only where the entry or lineage indicates that specialization.
c.bg: In the Throat, Deep Obyrith matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.bg: In the Throat, Obyriths matters when local suffering or bargains provide a stable foothold, turning social conflict into a biological and metaphysical hazard.

c.eco: Fiends persists where its lower-planar appetite has purchase: souls, fear, contracts, blood, ruin, corruption, or violence. It does not simply fill an animal niche; it engineers conditions that feed its nature.
c.beh: Fiends behaviour follows its fiendish specialization: bargaining, predation, temptation, command, ambush, or mercenary service as described by the entry. It recognizes advantage and retreats from losses that do not feed its purpose.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include evil-aligned aura, unnatural heat or cold, horns or claws where present, resistance to mundane harm, planar scent, and the specific body marks named in appearance.
c.abilities: Fiends abilities may include teleportation, poison, fire, fear, disguise, contract magic, life-drain, or summoning only where the entry or lineage indicates that specialization.
c.bg: Fiends are a full kingdom of Outer Primordial Life: evil-aligned planar organisms whose physiology, society, and powers are organized by infernal law, abyssal entropy, mercenary evil, or other lower-plane substrates. In the Throat they appear as incursions, bargains, cult contacts, or deep-adapted populations rather than as material descendants.

c.g: Beings of the Upper Planes for whom good is constitutive rather than chosen. All share planar-travel capacity, immunity to disease and fear, and good alignment as a physical property. None are native to the Throat.
c.tax: Kingdom-rank upper-planar origin; good alignment as constitutive property; immunity to non-magical disease and fear; radiant energy affinity; native planar travel. Celestials are parallel to Fiends and Outsiders under Outer Primordial Life, not subordinate to either.
c.ap: Winged or animal-headed bipeds 2–4 m tall; luminous from within; features of supernatural regularity. Stranded forms show attenuated luminosity and accumulated mineral dust.

c.g: The theorized common ancestor of all celestial-kind, prior to the divergence into lawful (Archon), neutral (Guardinal), and chaotic (Eladrin-line) good lines. Inferred from shared alignment-essence and planar properties.
c.tax: Inferred: good alignment as constitutive property; planar travel; radiant energy affinity. Precise form unknown.
c.ap: Unknown. Inferred to have been roughly bipedal with a luminous quality. The specific morphological divergences are post-split.
c.eco: Proto-Celestial is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: Proto-Celestial behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include good-aligned aura, luminous or idealized anatomy, resistance to disease and fear, clear speech or telepathy, and the animal or heraldic features named in appearance.
c.abilities: Proto-Celestial abilities may include radiant force, healing, protective aura, flight, truespeech, fear suppression, or weapon sanctification according to the exact celestial grade.
c.bg: In the Throat, Proto-Celestial is recorded as intervention, omen, ally, or context taxon rather than as a resident ecology.

c.g: Lawful good outsiders of Mount Celestia — less humanoid than Angels, more variable in form, equally good by constitution.
c.tax: Lawful good outsider; Mount Celestia origin; good alignment as constitutive property. Distinguished from Angels by non-humanoid base forms and lawful emphasis.
c.ap: Variable: Lantern Archons are floating spheres of light; Hound Archons are canine-humanoid; Trumpet Archons are humanoid with perfect features and a literal trumpet.

c.g: The most numerous and least powerful Archon — floating spheres of golden light that serve as messengers, scouts, and spiritual anchors for Celestial operations on the material plane. Despite their small size and limited combat capacity, Lantern Archons are pleasant to be around in the specific way that bright warm light is pleasant, which is not coincidental. They communicate in musical tones rather than language.
c.tax: Lawful good Celestial of Lantern Archon grade; no solid form — floating sphere of light; communication by musical tone; minimal combat capacity (light ray); immune to non-magical weapons; morale-boosting presence within 30 m. The least powerful Celestial; the most commonly helpful.
c.ap: 30 cm diameter sphere of warm golden light; constant gentle movement; no features; produces a persistent musical tone that changes meaning by pitch and rhythm; the light it produces is equivalent to a good lantern but softer.
c.eco: Lantern Archon is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: Lantern Archon behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include good-aligned aura, luminous or idealized anatomy, resistance to disease and fear, clear speech or telepathy, and the animal or heraldic features named in appearance.
c.abilities: Lantern Archon abilities may include radiant force, healing, protective aura, flight, truespeech, fear suppression, or weapon sanctification according to the exact celestial grade.
c.bg: In the Throat, Lantern Archon is recorded as intervention, omen, ally, or context taxon rather than as a resident ecology.

c.g: Dog-headed bipedal warriors of Mount Celestia — the infantry of the Archon host and the variety most frequently sent to the material plane for direct intervention. They are genuinely kind beings that happen to be built like a large athletic human with a dog's head and the combat capability to match. They are trusted as much as any warrior-class Celestial and considerably more approachable.
c.tax: Lawful good Celestial of Hound Archon grade; dog-headed humanoid bipedal form; natural bite attack and weapon use; tracking capacity superior to mortal canines; immune to non-magical weapons; can communicate in all languages by standing lawful-good decree.
c.ap: 1.8 m tall; humanoid body with dog head (typically resembling a large mastiff or similar breed); the expression is alert, friendly, and very serious; carries a greatsword; armoured; moves with a precision that makes its dog-head seem entirely appropriate.
c.eco: Hound Archon is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: Hound Archon behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include good-aligned aura, luminous or idealized anatomy, resistance to disease and fear, clear speech or telepathy, and the animal or heraldic features named in appearance.
c.abilities: Hound Archon abilities may include radiant force, healing, protective aura, flight, truespeech, fear suppression, or weapon sanctification according to the exact celestial grade.
c.bg: In the Throat, Hound Archon is recorded as intervention, omen, ally, or context taxon rather than as a resident ecology.

c.g: The herald-class Archons — tall, blindingly beautiful humanoid Celestials who carry a literal silver trumpet whose sound can shake planes. They announce divine proclamations, signal the arrival of Celestial forces, and in combat produce a trumpet-blast that disrupts magic and stuns non-Celestials. They are polite about this.
c.tax: Lawful good Celestial of Trumpet Archon grade; humanoid form of supernatural beauty; trumpet as signature implement and combat weapon (magical disruption blast); heralding function in Celestial hierarchy; immune to non-magical weapons.
c.ap: 2.2 m tall; humanoid; features of supernatural perfection; silver trumpet carried at all times; white armour; the overall impression is of something that should be on a ceiling fresco rather than in a passage, which is accurate in both directions.
c.eco: Trumpet Archon is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: Trumpet Archon behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include good-aligned aura, luminous or idealized anatomy, resistance to disease and fear, clear speech or telepathy, and the animal or heraldic features named in appearance.
c.abilities: Trumpet Archon abilities may include radiant force, healing, protective aura, flight, truespeech, fear suppression, or weapon sanctification according to the exact celestial grade.
c.bg: In the Throat, Trumpet Archon is recorded as intervention, omen, ally, or context taxon rather than as a resident ecology.

c.g: Neutral good outsiders of Elysium — animal-headed humanoid Celestials embodying natural good.
c.tax: Neutral good outsider; Elysium origin. Distinguished by animal-head morphology and natural-good rather than lawful-good alignment.
c.ap: Humanoid bodies with animal heads: Ursinal (bear), Cervidal (stag), Equinal (horse), Leonal (lion). Uniformly powerful and uniformly kind.

c.g: Lion-headed Guardinals — the most powerful of the Guardinal host and the most directly martial. They embody the neutral-good principle of protection through strength rather than law: a Leonal does not wait for permission to defend the innocent. They are majestic, warm, and capable of disembowelling a fiend with their natural claws in a single pass.
c.tax: Neutral good Celestial of Leonal grade; lion-headed humanoid; roar capacity causing terror in evil-aligned creatures; natural claw attacks; immune to non-magical weapons; maximum Guardinal power grade.
c.ap: 2 m tall; lion-headed humanoid with mane; golden fur on the head and shoulders transitioning to human-toned skin below; the roar is audible and the evil-fear effect is not subtle.
c.eco: Leonal is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: Leonal behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include good-aligned aura, luminous or idealized anatomy, resistance to disease and fear, clear speech or telepathy, and the animal or heraldic features named in appearance.
c.abilities: Leonal abilities may include radiant force, healing, protective aura, flight, truespeech, fear suppression, or weapon sanctification according to the exact celestial grade.
c.bg: In the Throat, Leonal is recorded as intervention, omen, ally, or context taxon rather than as a resident ecology.

c.g: Bear-headed Guardinals — the scholars and sages of the Guardinal host, the ones who accumulate knowledge rather than acting on it directly. They are the Guardinals most interested in the planes from a research perspective and the ones most likely to appear somewhere as an independent agent pursuing a specific piece of information rather than on a formal mission.
c.tax: Neutral good Celestial of Ursinal grade; bear-headed humanoid; exceptional knowledge across planar history and natural philosophy; natural claw attacks; immune to non-magical weapons.
c.ap: 1.9 m tall; bear-headed humanoid; dark brown fur on head and shoulders; carries books or scrolls as a consistent feature; the expression is one of permanent engaged curiosity.
c.eco: Ursinal is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: Ursinal behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include good-aligned aura, luminous or idealized anatomy, resistance to disease and fear, clear speech or telepathy, and the animal or heraldic features named in appearance.
c.abilities: Ursinal abilities may include radiant force, healing, protective aura, flight, truespeech, fear suppression, or weapon sanctification according to the exact celestial grade.
c.bg: In the Throat, Ursinal is recorded as intervention, omen, ally, or context taxon rather than as a resident ecology.

c.g: Horse-headed Guardinals — the fastest of the Guardinal host and the ones responsible for communication and rapid response across the planes. They can travel enormous distances quickly and are the Guardinal variety most likely to pass through a location briefly on the way somewhere else. If an Equinal tells you something needs to happen, it generally needs to happen before it is gone.
c.tax: Neutral good Celestial of Equinal grade; horse-headed humanoid; movement speed greatly exceeding humanoid standard; immune to non-magical weapons; communication function within Guardinal host.
c.ap: 2.1 m tall; horse-headed humanoid; the horse-head is full and maned; lean body suggesting speed; moves with the impatient quality of something designed to be in motion.
c.eco: Equinal is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: Equinal behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.

c.g: The winged messengers and warriors of the Upper Planes — the most commonly encountered Celestial kind across the planes. Divided by power into Devas, Planetars, and Solars. The Fallen of the Throat are angel-lineage.
c.tax: Upper Planar origin; good alignment as constitutive property; winged bipedal base form; radiant energy manipulation; native planar travel.
c.ap: Winged bipeds 2–4 m tall; luminous from within; features of extreme regularity; armor of divine quality. The Fallen retain all features except luminosity (reduced) and planar travel (lost).

c.g: Celestials that descended and could not return — not fallen in the moral sense, but stranded. Planar connection thins with depth, luminosity fades, mineral dust accumulates on feathers until it is an inch thick. They continue their mission.
c.tax: Celestials showing attenuated planar connection due to deep-stone immersion: permanent mineral dust on wing-coverts, loss of plane-travel, reduced luminosity. Alignment and constitution unchanged.
c.ap: Same baseline as intact angels; feathers mottled grey-white; armor tarnished silver filmed with mineral dust; eyes still bright; the dust makes a soft sound when they move.

c.g: The Fallen who have accepted that return is not currently possible and have stopped trying by conventional means. Some have been here long enough that grey-folk who met them have grandchildren.
c.tax: Fallen Celestials with active mission persistence despite confirmed loss of planar-travel capacity.
c.ap: 3 m tall; wings grey-white; armor mineral-dusted silver; moves deliberately; speaks all known languages and several that are not.

c.g: The Sunken One. Descended to the Fogreach on a mission it never completed. Its location is known to the Stonesong Heralds and withheld from wider records. Answers questions if asked respectfully. Has very few questions left itself.
c.tax: Stranded Angel with mission-persistence behavior and full mineral deposition; no planar travel; all other celestial properties intact.
c.ap: 3 m; wings mottled grey with white leading edges; armor deep-tarnished silver with an inch of mineral dust on pauldrons; eyes still bright; the dust on its feathers chimes faintly when it moves.
c.eco: The Sunken One is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: The Sunken One behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.

c.abilities: The Sunken One abilities may include radiant force, healing, protective aura, flight, truespeech, fear suppression, or weapon sanctification according to the exact celestial grade.
c.bg: In the Throat, The Sunken One is recorded as intervention, omen, ally, or context taxon rather than as a resident ecology.

c.g: The militant arm of the Celestial hierarchy — Planetar-grade angels tasked with direct divine intervention in the material plane. Distinguished from standard Angels by their green skin, greater size, and the fact that they are sent rather than descending of their own accord. Where a Stranded Angel is here by accident, a Planetar is here on orders.
c.tax: Angels of Planetar-grade: intervention-mandate, green skin, large (3 m+), healing and combat capacity significantly exceeding Deva-grade. Distinguished from standard Angels by scale, mandate, and the absence of autonomy in their mission definition.
c.ap: 3 m; green skin; armoured; two massive feathered wings; carries a holy sword and does not put it down.

c.g: A Planetar on mandate. Not confirmed in the Gorge but its possibility is taken seriously by the Stonesong Heralds, who maintain a standard recognition-and-contact protocol last reviewed forty years ago and not tested since.
c.tax: Planetar of intervention-mandate classification; on-orders rather than autonomous; distinguished by mission-specificity of all behaviour.
c.ap: Militant Planetar has a form shaped by its own planar origin: record posture, limbs, face, aura, temperature, scent, voice, and how nearby matter or emotion reacts. Do not import traits from other planar branches unless they are named descendants of this entry.

c.g: The most powerful Celestials — not a phylum in the conventional sense but a grade so far above other angel-kind that the Heralds classify them separately. A Solar has never been confirmed in the Gorge. If one were, the event would be significant enough to reorganise the chapter.
c.tax: Celestials of maximum power grade: two-weapons (sword and bow), truesight, limited wish capacity, divine awareness across planes. Distinguished from all other Celestials by power-grade rather than morphology.
c.ap: 4 m tall; burning golden-white from within; four wings; the brightness makes direct observation functionally impossible for biological eyes without protection.

c.g: A Solar. Not present in the Gorge. The entry exists so that if one appears, there is a classification to hand. The Heralds maintain this entry as a formality and a prayer.
c.tax: Solar of standard-grade: maximum Celestial power, truesight, divine awareness, limited wish. The entry is precautionary.
c.ap: As per Order description. The entry author notes they have never seen one and hopes the entry remains a formality.

c.g: Chaotic good celestials of Arborea — fey-touched and seasonal in temperament, cycling between states of emotion with the intensity of weather. Not to be confused with the fey elves of the same name; these are true celestials whose primal alignment tends toward joy, beauty, and the fierce protection of both. The Gorge has no confirmed Eladrin but the Singing Fronds respond to their resonance frequency, which the Heralds find suggestive.
c.tax: Celestials of chaotic-good alignment with fey-adjacent primal affinity; seasonal emotional cycling between Summer, Autumn, Winter, and Spring aspects, each with distinct combat and social profile; elf-form as base morphology.
c.ap: Elf-formed celestials 1.7–1.9 m with seasonal variation: Summer aspect golden-luminous; Autumn aspect amber-warm; Winter aspect silver-cold; Spring aspect green-bright.

c.g: The Autumn Eladrin — the most frequently encountered Celestial variety at the edges of known deep space, as Autumn is the aspect of transition and they are drawn to thresholds. The Fogreach is the threshold between the Works and the wild.
c.tax: Eladrin of Autumn aspect; warm-amber luminosity; drawn to transitional spaces and liminal geography; melancholy temperament by celestial standards.
c.ap: 1.8 m; elf-formed; warm amber light from within; hair the colour of copper-leaf; expression of reflective sadness even when not engaged in anything reflective.
c.eco: Autumn Eladrin is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: Autumn Eladrin behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include good-aligned aura, luminous or idealized anatomy, resistance to disease and fear, clear speech or telepathy, and the animal or heraldic features named in appearance.
c.abilities: Autumn Eladrin abilities may include radiant force, healing, protective aura, flight, truespeech, fear suppression, or weapon sanctification according to the exact celestial grade.
c.bg: In the Throat, Autumn Eladrin is recorded as intervention, omen, ally, or context taxon rather than as a resident ecology.

c.bg: In the Throat, Eladrin (Celestial) is recorded as intervention, omen, ally, or context taxon rather than as a resident ecology.

c.eco: Celestials is sustained by upper-planar nature rather than local food webs, though it may eat or rest for interaction with mortals. It appears where duty, rescue, judgment, or planar mandate brings it.
c.beh: Celestials behaviour is purposeful and morally directed: guarding, teaching, warning, healing, fighting evil, or carrying messages according to its form. It is not domesticated by the Throat even when stranded there.
c.traitsText: Diagnostic traits include good-aligned aura, luminous or idealized anatomy, resistance to disease and fear, clear speech or telepathy, and the animal or heraldic features named in appearance.
c.abilities: Celestials abilities may include radiant force, healing, protective aura, flight, truespeech, fear suppression, or weapon sanctification according to the exact celestial grade.
c.bg: Celestials are a full kingdom of Outer Primordial Life: good-aligned planar organisms whose bodies express radiance, protection, judgment, mercy, heraldry, or idealized animal and humanoid forms. In the Throat they are usually intervention, omen, ally, relic-source, or stranded context rather than resident ecology.

c.g: Outsiders are the outer-planar kingdom reserved for beings of planar personhood that are neither Fiends nor Celestials. They include non-aligned, pre-aligned, vestigial, or otherwise exceptional outer entities whose bodies are expressions of metaphysical plane rather than ordinary material descent. The inferred common source for the whole outer branch remains Proto-Outsiders; this kingdom is the living or active taxonomic container for outer beings that do not belong to the fiendish or celestial kingdoms.
c.tax: Kingdom-rank outer-planar organisms or entities defined by constitutive planar essence without membership in the evil-aligned Fiends or good-aligned Celestials. Fey are excluded and placed under Inner-Primordial primal life. Proto-Outsiders is the inferred species-level stem taxon and is not the same entry as this kingdom.
c.ap: No single body plan is diagnostic for the kingdom. Members may appear as void-shaped vestiges, alignment-neutral planar bodies, impossible silhouettes, or metaphysical presences with anatomy secondary to planar condition. Species entries must supply the actual limbs, organs, surface textures, sensory marks, and skeletal or incorporeal evidence.

c.g: The sealed entity at the base of the Gorge — beneath the Cenotaph of tungsten and true ice at the Nadir. It is not a Fiend. Its lineage predates the Outsider classification entirely: it condensed from Outer Primordial dissolution at the point of greatest depth rather than differentiating into alignment-essence. The Cult of Nothing's theology holds that it is not evil but prior — a thing that existed before the concept of alignment was needed.
c.tax: Primordial Vestige: direct descent from Outer Primordial dissolution without the alignment-differentiation step that produced Outsiders. Distinguished from Fiends by predating the Outsider classification entirely; from Elementals by outer-plane (not inner-plane) origin. Unique classification — no confirmed related organisms. Cannot be classified under any Kingdom.
c.ap: Sealed. The Cenotaph of tungsten and true ice above the Nadir is the only confirmed surface expression of its containment. No visual record of the entity itself exists in the open Heralds archives. The Cult of Nothing's most sacred texts describe it only as 'the weight of the stone before the stone knew it was stone.' All direct observational records are classified or missing.
c.note: Not a Fiend. Not an Elemental. Not an Aberration in the conventional sense. The Cult of Nothing and the Stonesong Heralds agree on its location and on the absolute necessity of the Cenotaph. They agree on little else. The Heralds classify it as Primordial Vestige (sui generis); the Cult calls it the First Silence; the grey-folk population calls it by no name at all.

c.g: A void-expression observed near the Cenotaph approach: a section of passage where the Stonesong simply stops — not blocked but absent, as if the concept of signal has been removed from that volume. Lasts 4–12 hours. The Cult marks each occurrence. The Heralds mark each in three separate classification records and have not reconciled them.
c.tax: Echo of the Nothing-Below manifesting as localized Stonesong absence and bioluminescence suppression. Not a biological organism; classified here as the closest known material expression of the Primordial Vestige.
c.ap: A volume approximately 2×2×4 m of passage where the Stonesong relay fails completely and all bioluminescence within is suppressed. The air inside is still and slightly cooler than ambient. Stone surfaces show no physical change.
c.eco: Void-Expression occupies the niche implied by its own origin, anatomy, and recorded habitat. For field use, note diet or energy source, reproduction or formation, shelter, lifecycle, and what evidence remains after it passes.
c.beh: Void-Expression behaviour should be read from its own body and origin: how it moves, feeds, responds to threat, treats kin or rivals, and repeats habits in the field.
c.traitsText: Void-Expression traits include the size, senses, resistances, movement, intelligence, vulnerabilities, and diagnostic field marks belonging to this entry alone. Recorded flags: Gorge-present. Trait tags: Nadir-zone tolerance.
c.abilities: Void-Expression abilities are the natural, supernatural, magical, psionic, or extraordinary capacities actually supported by its own description. Text indicates: stone.
c.bg: The Throat records Void-Expression for field relevance: distribution, danger, ecological role, fossil meaning, cultural contact, or comparison value. Status: Gorge-present.

c.g: Members of the Cult of Nothing who have spent sufficient time near the Cenotaph to show measurable changes — localized absence-phenomena identical to those of Void-Shaped Aberrations, but in living humanoids who chose the proximity. They remain alive and functional. The absent sections are not painful and do not bleed.
c.tax: Living Humanoids with localized absence-events caused by proximity to the Nothing-Below. Classified here as an expression of Primordial Vestige influence rather than as Undead (they are alive) or Aberration (the cause is not Far Realm). Distinguished from Void-Shaped by having sought the exposure voluntarily.
c.ap: Grey-folk of standard form with one or more sections simply absent — a finger, a patch of scalp, a forearm section — bounded by perfectly smooth edges that do not bleed or scar. The affected areas are cold to the touch of others but reportedly not felt by the Cult-Touched themselves.

c.g: A Cult-Touched whose absence-events have progressed to where their Stonesong connectivity is measurable to the Heralds relay network — they appear briefly as a signal-gap that moves with the intentionality of a person. The Heralds find this extremely disturbing. The Cult considers it an honor.
c.tax: Cult-Touched with relay-network-detectable absence field; distinguishable from standard Cult-Touched by Stonesong-null signature that registers on standard relay equipment.
c.ap: Grey-folk with approximately 15% of body volume absent in distributed sections; moves normally; eyes track normally; produces a faint cold radiation detectable at arm range.
c.eco: Signal-Null Touched occupies the niche implied by its own origin, anatomy, and recorded habitat. For field use, note diet or energy source, reproduction or formation, shelter, lifecycle, and what evidence remains after it passes.
c.beh: Signal-Null Touched behaviour should be read from its own body and origin: how it moves, feeds, responds to threat, treats kin or rivals, and repeats habits in the field.
c.traitsText: Signal-Null Touched traits include the size, senses, resistances, movement, intelligence, vulnerabilities, and diagnostic field marks belonging to this entry alone. Recorded flags: Gorge-present. Trait tags: Nadir-zone tolerance.
c.abilities: Signal-Null Touched abilities are the natural, supernatural, magical, psionic, or extraordinary capacities actually supported by its own description. Text indicates: cold, fly, stone.
c.bg: The Throat records Signal-Null Touched for field relevance: distribution, danger, ecological role, fossil meaning, cultural contact, or comparison value. Status: Gorge-present.

c.eco: Outsiders do not participate in the Throat's material food webs unless stranded or incarnated. Their ecology is planar: they persist through resonance, oaths, vestigial pressure, conceptual terrain, or the remaining physics of an outer plane. When present in the Gorge they alter routes, cult practice, bargains, and hazard conditions rather than forming ordinary breeding populations.
c.beh: Outsider behavior should be read from the specific plane or vestige involved, not copied from fiends or celestials. Some negotiate, some observe, some exert pressure simply by proximity, and some behave less like animals than like localized laws that have learned to notice intruders.
c.traitsText: Common field traits include resistance to mundane environmental decay, planar aura, atypical death conditions, and bodies whose boundaries may be metaphysical rather than anatomical. These are kingdom-level tendencies only; each subordinate species must define its own diagnostic marks.
c.abilities: Outsider abilities may include planar persistence, aura effects, pact pressure, absence phenomena, or localized alteration of space, memory, or alignment. They do not automatically possess fiendish fire immunity, celestial healing, or fey glamour unless a subordinate entry specifically records those powers.
c.bg: In the Throat, the Outsider kingdom exists mainly to classify the Nothing-Below and similar outer-planar exceptions without forcing them into Fiend or Celestial ancestry. It preserves the distinction between a true kingdom of exceptional outer beings and the inferred Proto-Outsiders stem species.

c.eco: Outer Primordials occupies the niche implied by its own origin, anatomy, and recorded habitat. For field use, note diet or energy source, reproduction or formation, shelter, lifecycle, and what evidence remains after it passes.
c.beh: Outer Primordials behaviour should be read from its own body and origin: how it moves, feeds, responds to threat, treats kin or rivals, and repeats habits in the field.
c.traitsText: Outer Primordials traits include the size, senses, resistances, movement, intelligence, vulnerabilities, and diagnostic field marks belonging to this entry alone. Recorded flags: fossil or inferred, theorized.
c.abilities: Outer Primordials abilities are the natural, supernatural, magical, psionic, or extraordinary capacities actually supported by its own description.
c.bg: Outer Primordial Life matters to the Throat because its kingdoms can intrude, bargain, become stranded, or leave transformed descendants without sharing ancestry with material beasts, plants, dragons, humanoids, or Far-Realm aberrations.
c.note: Promoted from Kingdom to Domain by cosmological placement. Kingdoms and lower ranks beneath this branch remain ordinary taxological entries within the outer-planar domain.

c.g: Far-Realm Life is the domain of organisms and quasi-organisms whose origin lies outside the ordinary Inner, Outer, Material, Mutagenic, and Deific systems. Its kingdoms are organized by body logic and mode of intrusion: dominion over minds, sovereignty of eyes, dream-lure predation, choral flesh, stone-flesh growth, rooted mouths, and abolethid memory empires.
c.tax: Domain-tier origin system for extra-cosmological life. Its subordinate kingdoms are not treated as one generic Far-Realm bucket; each kingdom is defined by shared anatomy, cognition, reproduction, and field signs.
c.ap: No single Far-Realm silhouette exists. The domain includes brains with servants, floating eyes, mucus emperors, stone-flesh colonies, many-mouthed masses, and rooted ambushers. A field entry must identify the specific kingdom before describing anatomy.
c.eco: Far-Realm ecology is intrusion ecology. These lifeforms enter, seed, dream, infect, bud, colonize water, inhabit stone, or enslave minds rather than sharing a common material food chain.
c.beh: Behavior across the domain is alien but not interchangeable. Dominion Minds administer, Ocular Sovereigns watch, Dream Lures bait, Choral Amorphs aggregate, Stoneborn wait, Rooted Maws ambush, and Abolethids remember and rule.
c.traits: Shared traits include incompatible anatomy, psionic resonance, resistance to ordinary taxonomic diagnosis, and a tendency to use local bodies or geology as scaffolding rather than as inherited form. Specimens may possess eyes without optic nerves, nerves without brains, digestive cavities without mouths, or memories that survive the destruction of the organism that held them.
c.abilities: Far-Realm abilities must be assigned at kingdom or lower rank. Telepathy, gaze-rays, mucus transformation, stone assimilation, paralysis, and dream-lures are not universal Far-Realm powers.
c.bg: This domain explains why carrion crawlers, ropers, beholders, mind flayers, aboleths, and mouthers can all be outside ordinary descent while still belonging to different high-level kingdoms.

c.g: Dominion Minds are Far-Realm organisms whose primary body is a command system: brain, parasite, colony, or predatory intelligence arranged to seize other nervous systems. They include illithid lines, elder-brain colonies, intellect devourers, and related cerebral vermin, but do not include beholders merely because both are intelligent aberrants.
c.tax: Kingdom of Far-Realm life defined by nervous-system predation, psychic communication, memory feeding, enthrallment, or brain replacement. A member of this kingdom is recognized by what it does to thought and agency, not by eye number, stone camouflage, mucus, or tentacles alone.
c.ap: Bodies in this kingdom put neural tissue at the center of the silhouette. Some are tall illithid bipeds with facial tentacles, some are exposed-brain quadrupeds, and some are sessile masses of wet thought, but all show oversized sensory or cerebral organs and reduced concern for ordinary animal symmetry.
c.eco: Dominion Minds persist by colony maintenance, host capture, tadpole or larval development, brain consumption, psychic enthrallment, and the conversion of prey into labor or breeding infrastructure. Their food chains are political as much as metabolic because a captured mind becomes habitat.
c.beh: They are calculating, territorial, and administrative. Even solitary species behave as if every encounter is a contest of ownership: who commands, who remembers, who is permitted to remain a self, and who becomes material for the colony.
c.traitsText: Stable traits include telepathy, high intelligence, neural sensitivity, resistance to ordinary morale pressure, preference for hidden colonies, and field signs such as missing brains, compulsive thralls, damp cerebral residue, and orders repeated by victims after the speaker is gone.
c.abilities: Common powers include domination, mind blast, memory extraction, psychic stunning, brain consumption, host detection, and in some lines the physical replacement or piloting of another creature's nervous system. These powers are confined to Dominion Minds and should not be copied to ocular or lithic kingdoms.
c.bg: In the Throat, Dominion Minds matter because a route can be conquered without visible war. A safe trading station, a quiet clan, or a helpful guide may already be part of a cerebral colony before any monster is seen.

c.g: The Mind Flayer lineage is an aberrant colonizing intelligence that uses humanoid bodies as both food and reproductive substrate. It is not a humanoid offshoot; the humanoid shape of an adult illithid is the result of parasitic occupation and remodeling.
c.tax: Placed under Aberrations because its defining life cycle begins with a tadpole-like invasive larva that consumes and rewrites a host brain, producing an adult body directed by illithid neural architecture. The lineage is linked by psionic heredity and elder-brain memory, not by material-plane humanoid descent.
c.ap: Illithids retain a humanoid torso only because the host frame is useful. The head becomes smooth, noseless, and cephalopod-like, with four feeding tentacles around a rasping mouth. Fingers lengthen, posture becomes still and economical, and the skull houses neural tissue no longer arranged like the original host brain.
c.note: Far Realm organisms occupy a fundamentally different cosmological position than Outer Plane beings. The Outer Planes differentiated from the Outer Primordials and are thus part of the same cosmological tree as the material plane. The Far Realm is not on this tree at all — it is a region exterior to both the inner and outer planes, possibly predating their formation, in which the laws of form and causality do not reliably apply. In this sense Far Realm Aberrations share their ontological category with the Primordial Vestiges: both are external to the evolutionary tree that produces all Kingdoms. The critical difference is that Vestiges are embedded remnants from before the tree formed; Far Realm organisms arrive through or around it from outside.

c.g: The colonial anchor of a Mind Flayer community — a vast brain-mass suspended in a brine pool, connecting telepathically to every Illithid in its colony and serving as their collective memory, strategic intelligence, and ultimate authority. The Elder Brain does not act in the physical world; it thinks, and its colony acts. The one confirmed in the deep Untamed pool is small by Elder Brain standards, suggesting a recently established colony. It is aware of the grey-folk. It has not acted on that awareness yet.
c.tax: Mind Flayer colonial anchor organism; brain-mass in brine suspension; telepathic network covering colony range (typically 5 km); no locomotive capacity; distinguished from individual Illithids by colonial-anchor role and vastly greater psionic range and power. The oldest Elder Brains are theorised to have consumed enough Illithid consciousnesses to constitute a form of immortality.
c.ap: A brain approximately 3–4 m diameter suspended in a brine pool, visibly pulsing; grey-purple; covered in writhing cilia; no discernible features in the conventional sense; the psionic field it generates is detectable by sensitive individuals as a pressure behind the eyes and a growing certainty that something knows where you are.

c.g: The Elder Brain of the Gorge colonial establishment — small, recently arrived, and currently in the phase of growth where it is assessing its environment rather than acting on it. The Stonesong resonance signature it produces was first recorded four years ago. The Heralds have been monitoring it since. Its psionic range is currently estimated at 2 km. That estimate increases each time they update it.
c.tax: Elder Brain of nascent-colony stage; Gorge-Untamed brine-pool residency; psionic range currently 2 km and expanding; distinguished from mature Elder Brains by limited colony size (estimated 6–12 Illithids) and absence of confirmed aggressive action toward non-colonial organisms.
c.ap: As Elder Brain family description; approximately 2 m diameter (smaller than mature specimens); located in a brine pool in the deep Untamed that the Heralds have not published the coordinates of. The pool is warm. The brine is slightly luminescent. Do not drink the brine.
c.eco: Gorge Elder Brain functions as a localized aberrant pressure rather than as a normal population. Its feeding, shelter, and reproduction follow the body described for this entry: lures bait prey, mineral-flesh forms colonize stone, colonial minds farm captives, and gaze-bodied forms arrange space around sight. Evidence in the field is therefore read from the exact remains it leaves, not from assumptions borrowed from animals or humanoids.
c.beh: Gorge Elder Brain behaves according to the alien logic of the Elder Brain branch. It should be expected to solve problems through its own sensory apparatus: waiting if built as a lure, coordinating if linked to a colony, watching if eye-dominated, burrowing through thought if psionic, or reshaping stone and flesh if mineralized. The entry should never be read as a generic monster with unusual decoration.
c.traitsText: Gorge Elder Brain traits include the size, senses, resistances, movement, intelligence, vulnerabilities, and diagnostic field marks belonging to this entry alone. Recorded flags: Gorge-present. Trait tags: aquatic respiration or movement.
c.abilities: Gorge Elder Brain uses abilities consistent with its own form and lineage: psionic pressure where neural tissue dominates, lure-mimicry where the body presents bait, gaze effects where eyes are weaponized, engulfing corrosion where mouths multiply, or mineral-flesh persistence where stone has been recruited into the body. No abilities are inferred from unrelated Far-Realm kingdoms unless named by the entry itself.
c.bg: In the Throat, Gorge Elder Brain is significant because it shows how Far-Realm intrusion adapts to a particular local condition rather than to ordinary descent. Its presence should prompt investigation of nearby dreams, cult sites, psionic residue, impossible mineral growth, vanished expeditions, or Nadir pressure effects.
c.traits: Diagnostic traits for Gorge Elder Brain are taken from its local anatomy: the number and placement of mouths, eyes, limbs, pseudolimbs, feeding surfaces, psychic organs, mineralized tissues, and colony links. These traits distinguish it from material monstrosities by showing a body assembled around aberrant function rather than inherited animal architecture.

c.g: The individual Mind Flayers of the Gorge colonial establishment — currently numbering an estimated 6–12 individuals operating within range of the Elder Brain. Colonial Illithids in an establishing colony behave differently from those in a mature one: they are cautious, surgical, and patient. They have not yet harvested a grey-folk brain. The Heralds believe this is strategy rather than restraint.
c.tax: Mind Flayer of colonial-establishment operational phase; subordinate to Encephalon profundus; distinguished from mature-colony Illithids by cautious non-aggressive posture and surgical intelligence-gathering focus.
c.ap: As Mind Flayer standard description: 1.8 m; grey-blue skin; four facial tentacles; large domed cranium; robes of deep colour; moves with absolute silence. In the Gorge they have been observed (once, at distance) wearing grey-folk clothing over their robes, which the Heralds find more alarming than the robes.

c.g: A standard colonial Mind Flayer of the Gorge establishment. Observed by the Bellowsmiths at a passage junction in the deep Untamed: it was standing still, observing a Troglodyte group without the Troglodytes' awareness. It did not act. When the Bellowsmith observer moved, it turned and looked directly at them through a passage wall. The Bellowsmith has been on extended personal leave since.
c.tax: Standard colonial Illithid; intelligence-gathering phase; one confirmed grey-folk-observer awareness event; has not yet fed on any confirmed Gorge resident.
c.ap: Standard Mind Flayer morphology; wearing grey-folk mining-clothes over standard Illithid robes, imperfectly fitted; the four facial tentacles are difficult to conceal and are not being particularly concealed.

c.g: An Ulitharid — the rare elite-grade Mind Flayer, distinguished by six rather than four facial tentacles and significantly greater psionic capacity. One is confirmed in the Gorge colony. In a mature colony an Ulitharid typically splits off to establish a new Elder Brain, becoming the anchor of a daughter-colony. The Heralds' current hypothesis is that the Gorge Elder Brain was established from an Ulitharid that descended specifically to found it.
c.tax: Elite Illithid of six-tentacle grade; psionic capacity significantly exceeding standard Illithid; colonial leadership-class; the individual who may have initiated the Gorge colonial establishment.
c.ap: As standard Illithid but 1.9 m; six facial tentacles rather than four; carrying no disguise; the psionic field it generates is detectable without special sensitivity as a mild persistent headache and the feeling of being considered.

c.g: What happens when an Elder Brain dies or is destroyed before its tadpole-pool of developing Mind Flayers has been implanted: the tadpoles survive by consuming one another, until one remains, massively overfed and without the cognitive structuring that implantation into a humanoid host would have provided. The result is a Neothelid — a creature of enormous size, overwhelming psionic power, and no social cognition whatsoever. The Gorge Neothelid is not from the current colonial establishment. It predates it by an estimated century, which implies a previous colonial attempt that failed. The current Illithid colonists are presumably aware of it. They have not mentioned it.
c.tax: Failed-implantation Mind Flayer tadpole of maximum survivor-from-pool size; 30–50 m body; overwhelming psionic capacity without social or cognitive organisation; no colonial affiliation; predatory toward any biological organism including Illithids. Distinguished from Elder Brain by locomotive capacity and the absence of any strategic intelligence.
c.ap: 30–50 m worm-form; purple-grey; four massive tentacles at the mouth end; the psionic field it radiates causes disorientation at 30 m, confusion at 15 m, and incapacitation at 5 m. Moves through the deepest Untamed passages by dissolving the rock ahead of it with a caustic secretion. The dissolved-rock passages it leaves are identifiable: smooth-walled, circular cross-section, and absolutely silent.

c.g: The Gorge Neothelid — a century-old survivor from a failed first colonial attempt. Its circular-section dissolved passages in the deep Untamed were initially catalogued by the Heralds as an unusual geological formation before the connection to failed Illithid colonisation was established. It has consumed at least three known Gorge organisms of megafaunal size based on the absence of those organisms from previously confirmed territory records. The current Illithid colonists route their operations around its passages with a care that the Heralds find informative.
c.tax: Neothelid of confirmed century-plus Gorge residency; passage-dissolution locomotion confirmed by passage-geometry analysis; three confirmed megafaunal predation events by absence-of-organism record; current Illithid colonists confirmed-aware by routing behaviour.
c.ap: Confirmed indirectly only. The passages attributed to it are smooth-walled, 3–4 m diameter, and circular in cross-section. One Heralds expedition member reported 'something moving at the edge of lamplight at the far end of a long straight passage' before the expedition turned back. The thing they saw was purple-grey and very large.

c.eco: Their ecology is colonial predation. Brains are food, memory, currency, and sometimes breeding infrastructure. A stable colony requires hidden chambers, controlled captives, brine pools, thralls for surface work, and enough secrecy that prey populations do not flee before harvest.
c.beh: Mind flayers behave like administrators of appetite. They prefer domination to open battle, enthrallment to negotiation, and long-term food security to dramatic slaughter. Individual personalities exist, but they develop inside a culture where another creature’s mind is treated as a harvestable organ.
c.traitsText: Mind Flayer lineage traits include the size, senses, resistances, movement, intelligence, vulnerabilities, and diagnostic field marks belonging to this entry alone.
c.abilities: Core abilities include telepathy, mind blast, domination, psychic extraction, thrall conditioning, and surgical use of living brains. Their most dangerous power is strategic: they can convert captured enemies into tools before the enemy’s allies know a battle has begun.
c.bg: In the Throat, an illithid sign is never just one monster. Missing persons, obedient witnesses, wet stone corridors, and headaches shared by an entire watch-post may indicate the edge of a colony whose true body is hidden deeper in brine and memory.
c.traits: Traits include psionic communication, tentacular feeding anatomy, high manipulative intelligence, light-sensitive subterranean habits, and dependence on brain consumption. Colonial forms add memory continuity through elder brains and specialized castes such as ulitharids and neothelids.

c.g: Intellect Devourers are brain-shaped predatory aberrations that treat skulls as burrows and identities as camouflage. They are associated with illithid colonies but remain distinct organisms: small, fast, and specialized for replacing a victim from inside.
c.tax: Placed under Aberrations because their body is a mobile neural organ with clawed legs and psionic predation, not a natural animal. Their reproductive and command relationships usually depend on stronger aberrant colonies, especially mind flayers.
c.ap: An intellect devourer resembles an exposed brain walking on four bestial legs. The surface folds pulse like wet grey muscle; the legs are sinewy and clawed; no face is visible because the creature senses by psionic contact and vibration rather than ordinary eyes.
c.note: Like Mind Flayers, Intellect Devourers are of Far Realm origin — not indigenous to the underdark, present there by colonisation.

c.g: A scout-grade servant for a Mind Flayer colony not yet confirmed in the Gorge. The Beast-Bonded track sightings as the most reliable Mind Flayer early-warning indicator; seven confirmed sightings in the last two decades, direction of travel consistently downward.
c.tax: Intellect Devourer of standard scout-function grade; confirmed Gorge-penetration sightings; movement direction consistent with colonial establishment approach.
c.ap: 20 cm; brain-with-legs; clawed; fast; seen primarily as a flash of pink-grey in lamplight at floor level moving away.
c.eco: Gorge Intellect Devourer functions as a localized aberrant pressure rather than as a normal population. Its feeding, shelter, and reproduction follow the body described for this entry: lures bait prey, mineral-flesh forms colonize stone, colonial minds farm captives, and gaze-bodied forms arrange space around sight. Evidence in the field is therefore read from the exact remains it leaves, not from assumptions borrowed from animals or humanoids.
c.beh: Gorge Intellect Devourer behaves according to the alien logic of the Intellect Devourers branch. It should be expected to solve problems through its own sensory apparatus: waiting if built as a lure, coordinating if linked to a colony, watching if eye-dominated, burrowing through thought if psionic, or reshaping stone and flesh if mineralized. The entry should never be read as a generic monster with unusual decoration.
c.traitsText: Gorge Intellect Devourer traits include the size, senses, resistances, movement, intelligence, vulnerabilities, and diagnostic field marks belonging to this entry alone. Recorded flags: context-only.
c.abilities: Gorge Intellect Devourer uses abilities consistent with its own form and lineage: psionic pressure where neural tissue dominates, lure-mimicry where the body presents bait, gaze effects where eyes are weaponized, engulfing corrosion where mouths multiply, or mineral-flesh persistence where stone has been recruited into the body. No abilities are inferred from unrelated Far-Realm kingdoms unless named by the entry itself.
c.bg: In the Throat, Gorge Intellect Devourer is significant because it shows how Far-Realm intrusion adapts to a particular local condition rather than to ordinary descent. Its presence should prompt investigation of nearby dreams, cult sites, psionic residue, impossible mineral growth, vanished expeditions, or Nadir pressure effects.
c.traits: Diagnostic traits for Gorge Intellect Devourer are taken from its local anatomy: the number and placement of mouths, eyes, limbs, pseudolimbs, feeding surfaces, psychic organs, mineralized tissues, and colony links. These traits distinguish it from material monstrosities by showing a body assembled around aberrant function rather than inherited animal architecture.

c.eco: They feed on intelligence and opportunity. A lone specimen hides in cracks, corpses, or battlefield clutter until it can stun a victim, consume or displace the brain, and use the body as a temporary shelter. Colonies employ them as infiltrators and cleanup hunters.
c.beh: They are ambush parasites rather than soldiers. The creature avoids fair fights, seeks isolated targets, and uses stolen bodies to pass doors, repeat familiar phrases, or walk back into camp before anyone notices the mind behind the eyes is gone.
c.traitsText: Intellect Devourers traits include the size, senses, resistances, movement, intelligence, vulnerabilities, and diagnostic field marks belonging to this entry alone. Recorded flags: context-only.
c.abilities: Abilities include psychic assault, intelligence damage, body theft, host puppeting, and telepathic coordination with illithid masters. Killing the host may not kill the devourer unless the skull is checked immediately.
c.bg: In the Throat, intellect devourers are feared because they turn trust into a route of infection. A single replaced scout can lead an entire expedition into an elder-brain corridor while wearing the face of someone everyone expected to see.
c.traits: Traits include tiny size, exposed neural body, quadrupedal movement, psionic senses, extreme stealth in cluttered stone, and vulnerability if forced into open terrain. The occupied host body may show only small errors: delayed reactions, wrong handedness, or a flatness behind the eyes.

c.g: Ocular Sovereigns are Far-Realm life arranged around the tyranny of sight. Their bodies make vision into rulership: central eyes, satellite eyes, hovering globes, gaze-rays, paranoia, and territory defined by line of sight rather than scent or spoor.
c.tax: Kingdom of Far-Realm life defined by eye-dominant anatomy and gaze-mediated power. Beholders, gauths, spectators, death tyrants, and lesser ocular servitors belong here; mind flayers do not, because psychic dominion and ocular sovereignty are separate origins.
c.ap: The typical body is radial or globular rather than vertebrate. A central orb, many eyestalks, a toothy mouth, and levitation replace limbs; even lesser servitors show eye-clusters, floating posture, and facial anatomy dominated by vision organs.
c.eco: Ocular Sovereigns reproduce through dreams, paranoia, magical budding, or the splitting of obsessive self-images. Their lairs are arranged as sight-machines: open killing galleries, reflected angles, anti-approach corridors, and trophy displays placed where every object can be watched.
c.beh: They are suspicious, self-mythologizing, and despotic. Most treat other organisms as mistakes unless useful as servants, statues, warnings, or proof that the sovereign eye is still central to the world.
c.traitsText: Traits include levitation, all-around vision, anti-ambush awareness, high territoriality, sleep disturbance, and skeletal absence or minimal internal hard parts. Corpses collapse into rubbery hide, lens matter, teeth, and stalk cartilage rather than a normal skeleton.
c.abilities: Abilities include antimagic cones, gaze rays, charm rays, disintegration, petrification, fear, sleep, death magic, necrotic ocular undeath in death tyrants, and lesser ocular alarms in servitor forms.
c.bg: In the Throat, Ocular Sovereigns turn vertical chambers into watchtowers and execution spaces. They are especially dangerous in open shafts where a floating body can command every ledge at once.

c.g: The Beholder lineage consists of aberrations whose bodies externalize paranoia, attention, and dream logic into anatomy. They are not floating animals with extra eyes; they are tyrannical perceptual centers that grow weapons wherever suspicion requires another line of sight.
c.tax: Placed under Aberrations because beholder forms arise from aberrant dreaming, reality pressure, and self-generating morphology rather than ordinary reproduction. Related forms share spherical central bodies, a dominant central eye, eyestalk arrays, levitation, and gaze-based powers.
c.ap: A beholder-line body is a floating globe of armored flesh with a huge central eye, a toothy lower mouth, and multiple flexible eyestalks rising from the crown or rear arc. There is no neck, spine, pelvis, or limb girdle. The body turns by drifting and rotating, keeping as many eyes as possible on suspected threats.
c.note: Far Realm organisms occupy a fundamentally different cosmological position than Outer Plane beings. The Outer Planes differentiated from the Outer Primordials and are thus part of the same cosmological tree as the material plane. The Far Realm is not on this tree at all — it is a region exterior to both the inner and outer planes, possibly predating their formation, in which the laws of form and causality do not reliably apply. In this sense Far Realm Aberrations share their ontological category with the Primordial Vestiges: both are external to the evolutionary tree that produces all Kingdoms. The critical difference is that Vestiges are embedded remnants from before the tree formed; Far Realm organisms arrive through or around it from outside.

c.g: Lesser Beholders — four-eyed variants that are summoned as guardians rather than arising from the Far Realm naturally. A Spectator is the result of a ritual using four Beholder eyes; it is bound to guard a location for 101 years and then disappears. After the binding period it becomes free but often lacks the context to do anything useful with freedom, and remains in place guarding something for additional centuries out of habit.
c.tax: Far Realm Aberration of lesser Beholder grade; four eye-stalks rather than ten; guardian-binding lifecycle; anti-magic eye-cone and four eye-ray attacks rather than ten; less paranoid than true Beholders, though paranoia is relative.
c.ap: 60 cm diameter sphere; central eye (antimagic cone); four eye-stalks; dark grey-purple; floats. The most distinguishing feature from a true Beholder is the distinct lack of the specific quality of malice that full Beholders radiate.

c.g: A Spectator in active guardian-binding, assigned to protect a specific location or hoard. Their binding period means that any Bound Spectator encountered has been there for up to 101 years, during which the thing they are guarding may have changed hands, been destroyed, or ceased to exist — none of which releases the binding. Spectators guarding empty rooms are a documented phenomenon.
c.tax: Spectator in binding-period operation; distinguished from free-roaming Spectators by the specific quality of purpose-driven attention they pay to the protected location.
c.ap: As Spectator family; eyes orient consistently toward the protected location even when addressing a visitor; the four eye-stalks track threats without the central eye leaving the protected area.
c.eco: Bound Spectator functions as a localized aberrant pressure rather than as a normal population. Its feeding, shelter, and reproduction follow the body described for this entry: lures bait prey, mineral-flesh forms colonize stone, colonial minds farm captives, and gaze-bodied forms arrange space around sight. c.g: Smaller Beholders, also sometimes called Death's Eyes — less powerful than true Beholders but no less paranoid, a combination that makes them especially dangerous in enclosed environments where their eye-rays have nowhere to miss. They are drawn to sources of magical energy as food, making underdark environments with magical infrastructure particularly attractive.
c.tax: Far Realm Aberration of gauth-grade Beholder; six eye-stalks; central eye drains magical energy rather than producing an antimagic field; feeds on magical energy rather than physical prey; less paranoid than true Beholders, primarily because their confidence in their own eye-rays reduces the need for paranoia.
c.ap: 50 cm diameter; six eye-stalks; yellow-green central eye that glows when draining magical energy; slightly smaller and less intimidating than a true Beholder until its eye-stalks unfold.

c.g: A Gauth detected in the deep Untamed by the Stonesong relay's unexplained signal-drain events in a specific passage section — Gauth feed on magical energy, and the Stonesong relay qualifies. The Heralds have not confirmed visual contact but have isolated the drain pattern to a moving source approximately 50 cm in diameter that avoids lamp-carried approaches. They are treating this as a Gauth sighting and planning accordingly.
c.tax: Gauth of confirmed underdark-passage residency by magical-energy-drain signature; Stonesong relay identified as food source; detected by relay-monitoring rather than direct observation.
c.ap: Unknown directly; source of signal-drain events is 50 cm in apparent diameter and mobile; avoids direct lamplight; the relay-drain pattern is consistent with Gauth magical-energy feeding behavior.
c.eco: Deep Gauth functions as a localized aberrant pressure rather than as a normal population.
